Test conflict detection: create project for items on dates, attempt second project with overlapping dates and same items, verify conflict error. Test conversion transaction rollback: simulate DB failure mid-conversion, verify opportunity status unchanged.

subtasks.title: Implement InventoryService: stock tracking, barcode scanning, check-out/check-in
subtasks.description: Implement the InventoryService gRPC server with stock level queries, barcode-based item lookup, and check-out/check-in transaction recording that atomically updates available stock counts.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `internal/service/inventory.go` implementing InventoryServiceServer.
2. GetStockLevel: return current available_stock for an inventory item by ID or list of items with filtering by store_id, category.
3. ScanBarcode: lookup inventory_item by barcode string, return item details and current stock level. Return NOT_FOUND for unrecognized barcodes.
4. RecordTransaction: accept inventory_item_id (or barcode), project_id, type (check_out/check_in/adjustment), quantity.
   - For check_out: within transaction, verify available_stock >= quantity, decrement available_stock, create inventory_transaction record. Return FAILED_PRECONDITION if insufficient stock.
   - For check_in: increment available_stock, create transaction record. Validate quantity doesn't exceed what was checked out for that project.
   - For adjustment: admin-only stock correction, requires appropriate header check.
5. CheckOut RPC on ProjectService delegates here: accept project_id + list of (item_id, quantity) pairs, process each as check_out transaction in a single DB transaction.
6. CheckIn RPC on ProjectService delegates here: accept project_id + list of (item_id, quantity) pairs, process each as check_in transaction.
7. Invalidate Valkey stock cache on any transaction (publish cache invalidation or direct delete).
8. Wire into gRPC server registration.
subtasks.test_strategy: Integration test check-out: set item stock to 10, check out 5, verify available_stock is 5 and transaction record exists. Check out 6 more, verify FAILED_PRECONDITION. Integration test check-in: check in 3 of previously checked-out items, verify stock incremented to 8. Barcode scan test: insert item with barcode 'ABC123', call ScanBarcode('ABC123'), verify correct item returned. Test unknown barcode returns NOT_FOUND.

subtasks.title: Implement CrewService: crew listing, assignment, and schedule conflict detection
subtasks.description: Implement the CrewService gRPC server with crew member listing, project assignment with role, and schedule conflict detection ensuring no crew member is double-booked.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `internal/service/crew.go` implementing CrewServiceServer.
2. ListCrew: return all active crew members with optional skill filtering.
3. AssignCrew: accept crew_member_id, project_id, role. Before assigning:
   - Fetch project date range.
   - Query rms.schedules for existing entries where crew_member_id has overlapping date ranges.
   - If conflict found, return FAILED_PRECONDITION with details of conflicting project.
   - If no conflict, create crew_assignment record and schedule entry.
4. ScheduleCrew: bulk assign multiple crew members to a project. Process each assignment, collecting conflicts and returning partial success information.
5. Wire into gRPC server registration.
subtasks.test_strategy: Test assign crew to project A (Jan 1-5), verify assignment created. Attempt assign same crew to project B (Jan 3-7), verify conflict error with project A details. Assign different crew to project B, verify success. Test ListCrew with skill filter returns only matching crew.

subtasks.title: Implement DeliveryService: scheduling, status tracking, and route optimization
subtasks.description: Implement the DeliveryService gRPC server with delivery scheduling, status updates, and nearest-neighbor route optimization algorithm for multi-stop delivery routes.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `internal/service/delivery.go` implementing DeliveryServiceServer.
2. ScheduleDelivery: accept project_id, scheduled_at, driver, vehicle, list of delivery addresses (with lat/lng). Create delivery record and delivery_route entries.
3. UpdateDeliveryStatus: update delivery status (scheduled→in_transit→delivered→completed). Validate transitions.
4. OptimizeRoute: implement nearest-neighbor heuristic:
   - Start from store/warehouse location.
   - At each step, select the unvisited stop closest to the current location (Haversine distance).
   - Assign sequence numbers and estimated ETAs based on average speed.
   - Return ordered list of stops with sequence and ETA.
5. Create `internal/geo/haversine.go` with Haversine distance calculation function.
6. Wire into gRPC server registration.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test Haversine: verify distance between known coordinates (e.g., NYC to LA) matches expected value within 1%. Unit test nearest-neighbor: given 5 stops, verify output sequence visits closest unvisited stop each time. Integration test: schedule delivery with 3 stops, call OptimizeRoute, verify sequence is ordered and ETAs are populated. Test status transitions: scheduled→in_transit→delivered works, delivered→scheduled returns error.

subtasks.title: Implement Google Calendar integration for project confirmation
subtasks.description: Create a Google Calendar client that creates/updates/deletes calendar events when projects are confirmed, using service account credentials from External Secrets.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `internal/integrations/gcal/client.go`.
2. Initialize Google Calendar API client using service account JSON credentials loaded from environment/secret mount path (e.g., /secrets/google-sa.json).
3. Define CalendarService interface: CreateEvent(project), UpdateEvent(project), DeleteEvent(projectID).
4. CreateEvent: build calendar event with:
   - Summary: project name/ID
   - Start/End: project event dates
   - Location: venue
   - Description: equipment list (from line items), crew assignments
   - Attendees: crew member emails (if available)
5. Store the Google Calendar event_id on the project record (add nullable gcal_event_id column via migration) for future updates/deletions.
6. Hook into ProjectService: after successful conversion or project confirmation, call CreateEvent. On project update, call UpdateEvent. On project cancellation, call DeleteEvent.
7. Make Google Calendar integration opt-in via feature flag environment variable (GCAL_ENABLED=true/false) to avoid failures in environments without credentials.
8. Handle API errors gracefully — log and continue, don't fail the project operation if Calendar API is unavailable.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test with mocked Google Calendar API: verify CreateEvent sends correct payload with venue, dates, crew, and equipment. Verify UpdateEvent sends updated dates. Verify DeleteEvent calls correct event_id. Test feature flag: when GCAL_ENABLED=false, no Calendar API calls are made. Test error handling: simulate Calendar API 500, verify project operation still succeeds with error logged.

subtasks.title: Implement NATS audit event publishing
subtasks.description: Create a NATS client wrapper that publishes fire-and-forget audit events to sigma1.audit.rms.* subjects on key business operations: opportunity creation, project conversion, check-out, and check-in.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `internal/messaging/nats.go` with NATSPublisher struct wrapping nats.Conn.
2. Connect to NATS using URL from sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap (NATS_URL env var).
3. Define audit event message format (JSON): {event_type, entity_id, entity_type, actor_id, timestamp, payload}.
4. Publish functions:
   - PublishOpportunityCreated(opportunityID, actorID) → subject `sigma1.audit.rms.opportunity.created`
   - PublishProjectConverted(projectID, opportunityID, actorID) → subject `sigma1.audit.rms.project.converted`
   - PublishCheckOut(projectID, items, actorID) → subject `sigma1.audit.rms.inventory.checkout`
   - PublishCheckIn(projectID, items, actorID) → subject `sigma1.audit.rms.inventory.checkin`
5. All publishes are fire-and-forget per D3: use nats.Publish (not request/reply), log errors but do not fail the business operation.
6. Integrate into service methods: call publish after successful database commit in OpportunityService.CreateOpportunity, ProjectService conversion, InventoryService CheckOut/CheckIn.
7. Make NATS connection optional — if NATS_URL is empty, log warning and skip publishing.
subtasks.test_strategy: Integration test: connect to test NATS server, subscribe to sigma1.audit.rms.>, create an opportunity, verify audit message received on correct subject with expected fields. Test fire-and-forget: disconnect NATS, create opportunity, verify operation succeeds without error. Test message format: verify JSON payload contains event_type, entity_id, timestamp.

subtasks.title: Implement Valkey caching for stock levels
subtasks.description: Add Valkey (Redis-compatible) caching layer for inventory stock levels with 30-second TTL, cache invalidation on transactions, and graceful fallback to database on cache miss or Valkey unavailability.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `internal/cache/valkey.go` with ValkeyCache struct wrapping go-redis client.
2. Connect using VALKEY_URL from sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap.
3. Cache key pattern: `rms:stock:{inventory_item_id}` → JSON {available_stock, total_stock, updated_at}.
4. GetStockLevel flow: check cache first → if hit, return cached value; if miss, query DB, populate cache with 30s TTL, return.
5. Invalidation: on any inventory transaction (check-out, check-in, adjustment), delete the cache key for affected item(s).
6. Wrap all Valkey operations with error handling: on Valkey connection error or timeout, fall through to database query. Log warning but don't fail.
7. Add Valkey connectivity to /health/ready check.
8. Integrate into InventoryService: inject ValkeyCache, use for GetStockLevel RPC.
subtasks.test_strategy: Test cache hit: populate cache, call GetStockLevel, verify no DB query made (use mock). Test cache miss: empty cache, call GetStockLevel, verify DB queried and cache populated. Test invalidation: check out items, verify cache key deleted. Test fallback: simulate Valkey connection failure, verify GetStockLevel still returns correct data from DB. Test TTL: set cache, wait 31 seconds, verify cache miss.

subtasks.title: Implement admin allowlist middleware and GDPR deletion endpoint
subtasks.description: Create grpc-gateway middleware that enforces admin allowlist via X-Service-Identity/X-User-ID headers per D11, and implement the GDPR customer data deletion endpoint that removes all customer-related data across rms tables.
subtasks.details:
1. Admin allowlist middleware (`internal/middleware/admin.go`):
   - Intercept HTTP requests on grpc-gateway mux.
   - Extract X-Service-Identity and X-User-ID headers.
   - For admin-only endpoints (GDPR delete, inventory adjustments), validate that X-User-ID is in an allowlist loaded from environment variable or ConfigMap (ADMIN_USER_IDS comma-separated).
   - Return 403 Forbidden if not authorized.
   - For regular endpoints, pass through (authentication handled upstream by API gateway).
2. GDPR DELETE endpoint (`internal/service/gdpr.go`):
   - Register REST handler: DELETE /api/v1/gdpr/customer/{customer_id}
   - Within a single database transaction:
     a. Delete all delivery_routes for deliveries linked to customer's projects.
     b. Delete all deliveries linked to customer's projects.
     c. Delete all crew_assignments linked to customer's projects.
     d. Delete all schedules linked to customer's projects.
     e. Delete all inventory_transactions linked to customer's projects.
     f. Delete all project_line_items linked to customer's projects.
     g. Delete all projects linked to customer's opportunities.
     h. Delete all opportunities where customer_id matches.
   - Use CASCADE or explicit ordered deletes to respect foreign keys.
   - Return 200 with count of deleted records per table.
   - Log GDPR deletion as audit event via NATS.
3. Register middleware on grpc-gateway HTTP mux.
subtasks.test_strategy: Test admin middleware: request GDPR endpoint with valid admin X-User-ID → 200; request with non-admin X-User-ID → 403; request without header → 403. GDPR integration test: create customer with 2 opportunities, 1 project with line items, transactions, crew assignments, and delivery; call DELETE endpoint; verify all data removed from all tables; verify response includes correct counts. Verify audit event published for GDPR deletion.

subtasks.title: Implement health checks and Prometheus metrics
subtasks.description: Add /health/live, /health/ready (checking PostgreSQL and Valkey connectivity), and /metrics endpoint exposing Prometheus metrics including gRPC method call counts, latency histograms, and database pool stats.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `internal/health/handler.go`:
   - `/health/live`: always return 200 {status: 'ok'} (process is alive).
   - `/health/ready`: check pgxpool.Ping() and valkey.Ping(). Return 200 if both succeed, 503 with details if either fails.
2. Create `internal/metrics/prometheus.go`:
   - Register gRPC server interceptors: grpc_prometheus.UnaryServerInterceptor for automatic method call counts and latency histograms.
   - Add custom metrics: rms_opportunity_conversions_total (counter), rms_inventory_transactions_total (counter by type), rms_stock_cache_hits_total, rms_stock_cache_misses_total.
   - Expose pgxpool stats as gauges: rms_db_pool_total_conns, rms_db_pool_idle_conns.
3. Register /health/live, /health/ready, and /metrics on the HTTP mux (alongside grpc-gateway routes).
4. Add gRPC interceptors to server creation in main.go.
subtasks.test_strategy: Verify /health/live returns 200. Verify /health/ready returns 200 when DB and Valkey are up. Stop PostgreSQL, verify /health/ready returns 503 with error detail. Verify /metrics returns Prometheus text format with grpc_server_handled_total metric. Make a few gRPC calls, verify /metrics shows updated call counts and latency buckets.

subtasks.title: Create Dockerfile and Kubernetes deployment manifests
subtasks.description: Build a multi-stage Dockerfile for sigma1-rms and create Kubernetes manifests including Deployment (2 replicas), Service, and ConfigMap references for namespace sigma1.
subtasks.details:
1. Dockerfile (`Dockerfile`):
   - Stage 1 (build): FROM golang:1.22 AS builder. WORKDIR /app. COPY go.mod go.sum ./. RUN go mod download. COPY . ./. RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -o /sigma1-rms ./cmd/server/.
   - Stage 2 (runtime): FROM gcr.io/distroless/base-debian12. COPY --from=builder /sigma1-rms /sigma1-rms. COPY migrations/ /migrations/. EXPOSE 50051 8080. ENTRYPOINT ["/sigma1-rms"].
2. Kubernetes Deployment (`k8s/deployment.yaml`):
   - namespace: sigma1, name: sigma1-rms
   - replicas: 2
   - Container: image sigma1-rms:latest, ports 50051 (grpc) and 8080 (http)
   - envFrom: configMapRef sigma1-infra-endpoints
   - env: additional secrets via secretRef for Google Calendar SA, NATS credentials
   - resources: requests 128Mi/100m, limits 512Mi/500m
   - livenessProbe: httpGet /health/live port 8080
   - readinessProbe: httpGet /health/ready port 8080
3. Kubernetes Service (`k8s/service.yaml`): ClusterIP service exposing ports 50051 and 8080.
4. Migration Job (`k8s/migration-job.yaml`): Kubernetes Job that runs migrations before deployment (initContainer or standalone job).
5. Add .dockerignore to exclude unnecessary files.
subtasks.test_strategy: Build Docker image successfully with `docker build`. Verify image runs and responds on both ports. Verify image size is reasonable (<50MB). Validate Kubernetes manifests with `kubectl apply --dry-run=client`. Verify envFrom references correct ConfigMap name. Verify probes point to correct endpoints.

title: Build Finance + Customer Vetting Service (Rex - Rust/Axum)
description: Implement the merged Finance and Customer Vetting service as a single Rust/Axum binary with separate route modules (per D7). Finance handles invoicing, payments, AR aging, payroll, multi-currency, Stripe integration, and tax calculation. Customer Vetting handles the automated background research pipeline (OpenCorporates, LinkedIn, Google Reviews, credit signals) with GREEN/YELLOW/RED scoring. Owns `finance` and `vetting` schemas in PostgreSQL.
details:
1. Initialize Rust workspace (or extend from Task 2 if shared workspace). Cargo.toml adds: `stripe-rs` (latest), `reqwest` (for external API calls), `rust_decimal`, `chrono`, `serde_json`.
2. Structure as single binary with two route modules:
   - `src/finance/` — all `/api/v1/finance/*`, `/api/v1/invoices/*`, `/api/v1/payments/*`, `/api/v1/payroll/*`, `/api/v1/currency/*` routes.
   - `src/vetting/` — all `/api/v1/vetting/*` routes.
   - Shared: database pool, Valkey connection, admin allowlist middleware, health/metrics.
3. SQLx migrations for `finance` schema: `invoices` (matching PRD Invoice struct: id, project_id, org_id, invoice_number auto-generated, status enum, issued_at, due_at, currency, subtotal_cents, tax_cents, total_cents, paid_amount_cents, stripe_invoice_id), `payments` (matching PRD Payment struct), `payroll_entries` (id, employee_id, period, hours, rate_cents, currency, type enum contractor/employee), `currency_rates` (from_currency, to_currency, rate, fetched_at).
4. SQLx migrations for `vetting` schema: `vetting_results` (matching PRD VettingResult struct: org_id, business_verified, opencorporates_data JSONB, linkedin_exists, linkedin_followers, google_reviews_rating, google_reviews_count, credit_score, risk_flags JSONB array, final_score enum, vetted_at), `credit_signals` (org_id, source, signal_data JSONB, fetched_at).
5. Finance endpoints (all per PRD):
   - `POST /api/v1/invoices` — create invoice from project_id. Calls RMS REST API to fetch project details (per D5 — no cross-schema join). Auto-generates invoice_number.
   - `GET /api/v1/invoices` — list with filters (status, date range, customer).
   - `GET /api/v1/invoices/:id` — detail with payments.
   - `POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/send` — mark as sent, trigger Stripe invoice creation if payment method is card.
   - `POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/paid` — record payment, update status.
   - `POST /api/v1/payments` — record payment against invoice.
   - `GET /api/v1/payments` — list payments.
   - `GET /api/v1/payments/invoice/:id` — payments for specific invoice.
   - `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/revenue?period=` — aggregate revenue by period.
   - `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/aging` — AR aging buckets (current, 30, 60, 90+ days).
   - `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/cashflow` — cash inflows/outflows by period.
   - `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/profitability` — per-project profitability (calls RMS API for project cost data).
   - `GET /api/v1/payroll?period=` — payroll summary.
   - `POST /api/v1/payroll/entries` — add payroll entry.
   - `GET /api/v1/currency/rates` — current cached rates.
6. Stripe integration (per D12): use `stripe-rs` for invoice creation, payment intent, webhook handling. Implement `/api/v1/stripe/webhook` for payment confirmations. Use idempotency keys on all Stripe calls. Verify webhook signatures.
7. Multi-currency: support USD, CAD, AUD, NZD. Currency rate sync as a background tokio task running every 6 hours, fetching from a free exchange rate API, caching in `currency_rates` table and Valkey (TTL 6h).
8. Tax calculation: configurable tax rules per region (GST/HST for Canada, sales tax for US). Store tax rules as config, apply during invoice creation.
9. Automated payment reminders: background task checks for overdue invoices daily, publishes reminder events to NATS `sigma1.notifications.payment_reminder` (fire-and-forget per D3).
10. Vetting endpoints:
    - `POST /api/v1/vetting/run` — accepts org_id, runs full pipeline asynchronously (spawns tokio task). Returns 202 with vetting job ID.
    - `GET /api/v1/vetting/:org_id` — get vetting results.
    - `GET /api/v1/vetting/credit/:org_id` — get credit signals.
11. Vetting pipeline implementation (all external calls use reqwest with 10s timeout per D7 caveat):
    - Step 1: OpenCorporates API — verify business registration, good standing, directors.
    - Step 2: LinkedIn company lookup — check existence, follower count.
    - Step 3: Google Reviews — fetch rating and review count.
    - Step 4: Credit signal lookup via commercial API.
    - Step 5: Weighted scoring algorithm → GREEN (score >= 80), YELLOW (50-79), RED (<50). Weights: business_verified 30%, online_presence 20%, reputation 25%, credit 25%.
12. GDPR endpoint: `DELETE /api/v1/gdpr/customer/{id}` — delete all invoices, payments, payroll entries, vetting results for customer. Log to `audit` schema.
13. Admin allowlist middleware (shared with finance routes) per D11.
14. Health/metrics: `/health/live`, `/health/ready`, `/metrics`.
15. Valkey: cache currency rates, vetting results (TTL 24h), invoice counts for dashboard.
16. NATS: publish audit events to `sigma1.audit.finance.*` and `sigma1.audit.vetting.*`.
17. Dockerfile: single multi-stage build producing `finance-vetting` binary.
18. Kubernetes Deployment: single Deployment `finance-vetting`, namespace `sigma1`, 2 replicas, envFrom `sigma1-infra-endpoints`.
test_strategy: 1. Unit test invoice creation: given project_id, verify invoice_number auto-generated, tax calculated correctly for Canadian GST (13% on $1000 = $130 tax_cents=13000). 2. Unit test AR aging: create invoices due 10, 40, 70, 100 days ago; verify aging report buckets correct. 3. Integration test Stripe: mock Stripe API, create invoice, send → verify Stripe invoice created with correct amount and currency; simulate webhook → verify invoice marked paid. 4. Integration test vetting pipeline: mock OpenCorporates (verified), LinkedIn (exists, 500 followers), Google Reviews (4.5, 50 reviews), credit (good) → verify final_score=GREEN. Mock OpenCorporates (not found) → verify final_score=RED. 5. Verify vetting external calls have 10s timeout: mock API with 15s delay, verify timeout error handled gracefully. 6. Multi-currency test: create invoice in CAD, record payment in USD, verify conversion uses cached rate. 7. GDPR test: create invoices and vetting results for customer, call DELETE, verify all data removed from both schemas. 8. Verify /api/v1/finance/* and /api/v1/vetting/* routes both work from single binary. 9. Invoice generation must complete in <5 seconds (benchmark test). 10. Verify payment reminder background task publishes to NATS for overdue invoices.
decision_points: Which free exchange rate API to use for multi-currency sync (e.g., exchangerate.host, Open Exchange Rates free tier, frankfurter.app)?
Which commercial credit signal API/provider to integrate for the vetting pipeline credit check step?
LinkedIn company lookup method: official LinkedIn Marketing API (requires partnership approval) vs. scraping proxy vs. alternative data provider?
Google Reviews data source: Google Places API (paid, requires API key with billing) vs. third-party reviews aggregator?
OpenCorporates API tier: free tier has rate limits — is a paid plan needed for production volumes?
Tax calculation approach: hardcoded regional rules in config file vs. integration with a tax API (e.g., TaxJar, Avalara) for accuracy across jurisdictions?
Stripe Connect vs. standard Stripe integration: does the platform bill on behalf of the business or does each org have their own Stripe account?

subtasks.title: Initialize Rust project structure with dual route modules and shared infrastructure
subtasks.description: Set up the Cargo workspace/binary with `src/finance/` and `src/vetting/` route module directories, shared Axum app builder, database pool (SQLx), Valkey connection pool, and configuration loading from environment variables (envFrom sigma1-infra-endpoints). Wire up the Axum router with nested route groups for `/api/v1/finance/*`, `/api/v1/invoices/*`, `/api/v1/payments/*`, `/api/v1/payroll/*`, `/api/v1/currency/*`, `/api/v1/vetting/*`, and `/api/v1/stripe/*`.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `Cargo.toml` with dependencies: axum 0.7, tokio (full features), sqlx (postgres, runtime-tokio-rustls, macros), stripe-rs, reqwest (json, rustls-tls), rust_decimal, chrono, serde/serde_json, uuid, tower-http (cors, trace), async-nats, redis (for Valkey), tracing/tracing-subscriber.
2. Create directory structure: `src/main.rs`, `src/config.rs`, `src/db.rs`, `src/valkey.rs`, `src/nats.rs`, `src/error.rs`, `src/finance/mod.rs`, `src/finance/routes.rs`, `src/finance/models.rs`, `src/finance/handlers/`, `src/vetting/mod.rs`, `src/vetting/routes.rs`, `src/vetting/models.rs`, `src/vetting/handlers/`, `src/middleware/`, `src/shared/`.
3. In `src/config.rs`: load DATABASE_URL, VALKEY_URL, NATS_URL, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, RMS_SERVICE_URL from env.
4. In `src/db.rs`: create SQLx PgPool with max_connections=10.
5. In `src/valkey.rs`: create redis ConnectionManager.
6. In `src/nats.rs`: create async-nats client connection.
7. In `src/main.rs`: build Axum app with shared AppState (pool, valkey, nats, config), nest finance and vetting routers, bind to 0.0.0.0:8080.
8. In `src/error.rs`: define AppError enum with IntoResponse impl for consistent JSON error responses.
subtasks.test_strategy: Verify the binary compiles and starts successfully with mock env vars. Confirm that requests to any nested route prefix return 404 (not connection refused), proving the router is wired. Verify AppState is accessible in a trivial health handler.

subtasks.title: Create SQLx migrations for the finance schema
subtasks.description: Write SQLx migration files for the `finance` schema including `invoices`, `payments`, `payroll_entries`, and `currency_rates` tables with all columns matching the PRD structs, appropriate enums, indexes, and foreign key constraints.
subtasks.details:
1. Create migration: `CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS finance;`
2. Create enum types: `finance.invoice_status` (draft, sent, paid, overdue, cancelled, void), `finance.payment_method` (card, bank_transfer, check, cash, other), `finance.payroll_type` (contractor, employee), `finance.currency` (USD, CAD, AUD, NZD).
3. Create `finance.invoices` table: id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), project_id UUID NOT NULL, org_id UUID NOT NULL, invoice_number TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, status finance.invoice_status DEFAULT 'draft', issued_at TIMESTAMPTZ, due_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, currency finance.currency NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USD', subtotal_cents BIGINT NOT NULL, tax_cents BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, total_cents BIGINT NOT NULL, paid_amount_cents BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, stripe_invoice_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). Indexes on org_id, status, due_at, invoice_number.
4. Create `finance.payments` table: id UUID PK, invoice_id UUID FK → finance.invoices(id), amount_cents BIGINT NOT NULL, currency finance.currency, method finance.payment_method, stripe_payment_intent_id TEXT, paid_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). Index on invoice_id.
5. Create `finance.payroll_entries`: id UUID PK, employee_id UUID NOT NULL, period TEXT NOT NULL (e.g. '2024-01'), hours DECIMAL NOT NULL, rate_cents BIGINT NOT NULL, currency finance.currency, type finance.payroll_type, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). Index on employee_id, period.
6. Create `finance.currency_rates`: id SERIAL PK, from_currency finance.currency, to_currency finance.currency, rate DECIMAL(18,8) NOT NULL, fetched_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). Unique constraint on (from_currency, to_currency). Index on fetched_at.
7. Create an auto-increment sequence for invoice_number generation: `finance.invoice_number_seq`.
8. Add updated_at trigger function for invoices.
subtasks.test_strategy: Run `sqlx migrate run` against a test PostgreSQL instance. Verify all tables exist in the finance schema with correct column types using `\d finance.invoices` etc. Insert sample rows into each table and verify constraints (unique invoice_number, FK from payments to invoices, enum validation).

subtasks.title: Create SQLx migrations for the vetting schema
subtasks.description: Write SQLx migration files for the `vetting` schema including `vetting_results` and `credit_signals` tables with all columns matching the PRD VettingResult struct, JSONB fields, and appropriate indexes.
subtasks.details:
1. Create migration: `CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS vetting;`
2. Create enum type: `vetting.risk_score` (GREEN, YELLOW, RED).
3. Create `vetting.vetting_results` table: id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), org_id UUID NOT NULL, business_verified BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false, opencorporates_data JSONB, linkedin_exists BOOLEAN, linkedin_followers INTEGER, google_reviews_rating DECIMAL(2,1), google_reviews_count INTEGER, credit_score INTEGER, risk_flags JSONB DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb, final_score vetting.risk_score, vetted_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). Index on org_id. Consider unique constraint on org_id if only latest result matters, or keep history.
4. Create `vetting.credit_signals` table: id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), org_id UUID NOT NULL, source TEXT NOT NULL, signal_data JSONB NOT NULL, fetched_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). Index on org_id, source.
5. Create `vetting.vetting_jobs` table for async job tracking: id UUID PK, org_id UUID NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' (pending, running, completed, failed), error_message TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Index on org_id, status.
subtasks.test_strategy: Run `sqlx migrate run` against a test PostgreSQL instance. Verify all tables exist in the vetting schema. Insert sample vetting_results with JSONB data, verify JSONB queries work. Verify enum constraint rejects invalid final_score values.

subtasks.title: Implement admin allowlist middleware, health endpoints, and metrics
subtasks.description: Build the shared admin allowlist middleware (per D11) that checks authenticated user against an allowed admin list, plus /health/live, /health/ready, and /metrics endpoints.
subtasks.details:
1. In `src/middleware/admin_allowlist.rs`: implement an Axum middleware layer that extracts the authenticated user ID (from a header like X-User-ID or JWT claim — depends on auth strategy) and checks it against a configured allowlist (loaded from env var ADMIN_ALLOWLIST as comma-separated UUIDs). Return 403 JSON error if not in allowlist.
2. In `src/middleware/mod.rs`: export the middleware.
3. In `src/shared/health.rs`: implement `GET /health/live` returning 200 `{"status": "ok"}` (always passes). Implement `GET /health/ready` that checks DB pool connectivity (sqlx::query `SELECT 1`) and Valkey ping. Returns 200 if both pass, 503 if either fails.
4. In `src/shared/metrics.rs`: set up prometheus metrics using `metrics` + `metrics-exporter-prometheus` crates. Track request count, request latency histogram, active connections. Expose at `GET /metrics` in Prometheus text format.
5. Wire health and metrics routes into the main router (not behind admin middleware). Wire admin middleware onto finance and vetting route groups as needed.
subtasks.test_strategy: Test admin middleware: send request with allowed user ID → 200; send with disallowed user ID → 403; send with no user ID → 401. Test /health/live always returns 200. Test /health/ready returns 200 when DB and Valkey are up, 503 when either is down (stop Valkey, verify 503). Test /metrics returns valid Prometheus format.

subtasks.title: Implement invoice CRUD endpoints with auto-number generation and tax calculation
subtasks.description: Build the invoice management handlers: POST create, GET list (with filters), GET detail, including auto-generated invoice numbers, cross-service RMS project data fetching, and regional tax calculation engine (GST/HST for Canada, sales tax for US).
subtasks.details:
1. In `src/finance/models.rs`: define Rust structs for Invoice, CreateInvoiceRequest, InvoiceListFilter (status, date_range, org_id), InvoiceResponse, with serde Serialize/Deserialize.
2. In `src/finance/tax.rs`: implement tax calculation engine. Define TaxRule struct (region, rate, name). Load tax rules from config/env (e.g. CA_ON_GST_RATE=0.13, CA_ON_HST_RATE=0.13, US_DEFAULT_TAX=0.0). Function `calculate_tax(subtotal_cents: i64, region: &str) -> (i64, String)` returns (tax_cents, tax_description). Support: Canada ON HST 13%, CA BC GST 5% + PST 7%, US states with configurable rates.
3. In `src/finance/services/rms_client.rs`: implement HTTP client to call RMS service (URL from config) at `GET {RMS_URL}/api/v1/projects/{id}` to fetch project details (customer org_id, name, value). Use reqwest with 5s timeout. Handle RMS being unavailable gracefully.
4. In `src/finance/handlers/invoices.rs`:
   - `POST /api/v1/invoices`: accept CreateInvoiceRequest (project_id, currency, region, line_items or use project value). Call RMS to get project details. Auto-generate invoice_number using sequence (format: INV-{YYYY}-{seq:06}). Calculate tax. Insert into finance.invoices. Return 201 with InvoiceResponse.
   - `GET /api/v1/invoices`: accept query params for status, date range, customer org_id. Build dynamic SQLx query with filters. Return paginated list.
   - `GET /api/v1/invoices/:id`: fetch invoice by ID with associated payments (LEFT JOIN or separate query). Return 404 if not found.
5. Wire routes in `src/finance/routes.rs`.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test tax calculation: verify CA ON HST 13% on $1000 subtotal = $130 tax (13000 cents). Verify US no-tax region = 0. Unit test invoice number generation: verify format INV-2024-000001. Integration test: mock RMS returning project data, POST /api/v1/invoices → verify 201, invoice stored with correct fields. GET list with status=draft filter → only draft invoices returned. GET /api/v1/invoices/:id → returns invoice with payments array. Invoice creation completes in <5 seconds.

subtasks.title: Implement invoice send and payment recording endpoints
subtasks.description: Build the endpoints for sending invoices (mark as sent), recording payments against invoices (POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/paid, POST /api/v1/payments), listing payments, and updating invoice paid_amount_cents and status transitions.
subtasks.details:
1. In `src/finance/models.rs`: define Payment, CreatePaymentRequest (invoice_id, amount_cents, currency, method), PaymentResponse.
2. In `src/finance/handlers/invoices.rs`:
   - `POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/send`: validate invoice exists and is in 'draft' status. Update status to 'sent', set issued_at to now(). (Stripe integration will be layered on in the next subtask.) Return updated invoice.
   - `POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/paid`: accept payment details. Validate invoice exists and status is 'sent' or 'overdue'. Insert payment record. Update invoice paid_amount_cents += payment. If paid_amount_cents >= total_cents, set status='paid'. Return updated invoice.
3. In `src/finance/handlers/payments.rs`:
   - `POST /api/v1/payments`: create payment record linked to invoice_id. Same logic as above for updating invoice amounts.
   - `GET /api/v1/payments`: list all payments with optional filters (date range, method).
   - `GET /api/v1/payments/invoice/:id`: list payments for a specific invoice.
4. Implement invoice status transition validation: draft→sent→paid/overdue→void. Reject invalid transitions with 422.
5. Wire routes.
subtasks.test_strategy: Test send: POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/send on draft invoice → status becomes 'sent', issued_at set. Send on already-sent invoice → 422. Test payment recording: POST payment of 50% → paid_amount_cents updated, status still 'sent'. POST remaining 50% → status becomes 'paid'. Test overpayment rejected. Test GET /api/v1/payments/invoice/:id returns correct payments. Test invalid status transitions return 422.

subtasks.title: Implement Stripe integration for invoice creation and webhook handling
subtasks.description: Integrate stripe-rs for creating Stripe invoices on send, handling payment intents, implementing the webhook endpoint with signature verification, and using idempotency keys on all Stripe API calls.
subtasks.details:
1. In `src/finance/services/stripe_client.rs`: create a Stripe client wrapper using stripe-rs with the STRIPE_SECRET_KEY from config.
2. Implement `create_stripe_invoice(invoice: &Invoice) -> Result<String>`: creates a Stripe Invoice object with line items matching the invoice total_cents and currency. Uses idempotency key derived from invoice.id. Returns stripe_invoice_id. Handles Stripe errors gracefully.
3. Implement `create_payment_intent(invoice: &Invoice) -> Result<String>`: creates a Stripe PaymentIntent for the invoice amount. Uses idempotency key.
4. Update `POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/send` handler: after marking as sent, call create_stripe_invoice(). Store returned stripe_invoice_id on the invoice record. If Stripe call fails, still mark as sent but log error and leave stripe_invoice_id null.
5. In `src/finance/handlers/stripe_webhook.rs`: implement `POST /api/v1/stripe/webhook`:
   - Extract raw body bytes and Stripe-Signature header.
   - Verify webhook signature using STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
   - Parse event type. Handle `invoice.paid`, `payment_intent.succeeded` events.
   - On payment confirmation: look up invoice by stripe_invoice_id, record payment, update status to paid.
   - Return 200 on success, 400 on signature failure.
6. Ensure all Stripe calls use idempotency keys (UUID v5 from invoice ID + operation type) to prevent duplicate charges.
7. Wire webhook route (NOT behind admin middleware — Stripe needs to reach it).
subtasks.test_strategy: Integration test with mocked Stripe API: POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/send → verify Stripe invoice creation called with correct amount and currency, stripe_invoice_id stored. Simulate webhook POST with valid signature for invoice.paid → verify invoice status updated to paid and payment record created. Simulate webhook with invalid signature → verify 400 returned. Verify idempotency: call send twice for same invoice → only one Stripe invoice created. Verify webhook route is accessible without admin middleware.

subtasks.title: Implement financial reports (AR aging, revenue, cashflow, profitability) and payroll endpoints
subtasks.description: Build the four financial reporting endpoints (AR aging buckets, revenue by period, cashflow, per-project profitability) and payroll management endpoints (list payroll, add entry).
subtasks.details:
1. In `src/finance/handlers/reports.rs`:
   - `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/aging`: query invoices where status IN ('sent','overdue'). Bucket by days overdue: current (0-30), 30-day (31-60), 60-day (61-90), 90+ day. Return JSON: {current: {count, total_cents}, thirty: {...}, sixty: {...}, ninety_plus: {...}}.
   - `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/revenue?period=monthly|quarterly|yearly&start=&end=`: aggregate paid invoice total_cents grouped by period. Use SQL date_trunc. Return array of {period, total_cents, currency}.
   - `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/cashflow?period=monthly&start=&end=`: inflows = sum of payments by period; outflows = sum of payroll_entries by period. Return {periods: [{period, inflows_cents, outflows_cents, net_cents}]}.
   - `GET /api/v1/finance/reports/profitability`: call RMS API to get project list with cost data. For each project, get sum of invoice paid amounts. Return [{project_id, revenue_cents, cost_cents, profit_cents, margin_pct}].
2. In `src/finance/handlers/payroll.rs`:
   - `GET /api/v1/payroll?period=2024-01`: list payroll entries for period. Include summary totals.
   - `POST /api/v1/payroll/entries`: create payroll entry. Validate required fields (employee_id, period, hours, rate_cents, currency, type).
3. Wire all routes.
subtasks.test_strategy: AR aging test: create invoices due 10, 40, 70, 100 days ago. GET aging → verify correct bucket counts and amounts. Revenue report: create 3 paid invoices across 2 months → verify monthly aggregation correct. Cashflow: create payments and payroll entries → verify inflows/outflows/net calculated correctly. Profitability: mock RMS returning project costs, create invoices → verify margin calculation. Payroll: POST entry → verify stored. GET with period filter → only matching entries returned.

subtasks.title: Implement multi-currency rate sync background task with Valkey caching
subtasks.description: Build the background tokio task that fetches exchange rates for USD/CAD/AUD/NZD every 6 hours from an external API, stores them in the currency_rates table, and caches them in Valkey with 6h TTL. Implement the GET /api/v1/currency/rates endpoint.
subtasks.details:
1. In `src/finance/services/currency.rs`: implement `fetch_exchange_rates()` using reqwest to call the configured exchange rate API. Fetch rates for all pairs among USD, CAD, AUD, NZD (base=USD, convert to others). Parse response JSON. Return Vec<CurrencyRate>.
2. Implement `sync_rates(pool: &PgPool, valkey: &ConnectionManager)`: call fetch_exchange_rates(), upsert into finance.currency_rates (ON CONFLICT (from_currency, to_currency) DO UPDATE SET rate=, fetched_at=now()). Also SET in Valkey with key `currency:USD:CAD` etc., TTL 6 hours.
3. In `src/finance/tasks.rs`: spawn a tokio::spawn background loop that runs sync_rates every 6 hours (tokio::time::interval). Run once on startup, then every 6h. Log errors but don't crash.
4. Implement `get_rate(from: Currency, to: Currency, valkey, pool) -> Decimal`: first check Valkey cache, fallback to DB query. Used by invoice creation for multi-currency support.
5. In `src/finance/handlers/currency.rs`: `GET /api/v1/currency/rates` → fetch all current rates from Valkey (fallback DB). Return JSON array of {from, to, rate, fetched_at}.
6. Wire the background task startup in main.rs after building the app. Wire the route.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test rate parsing from mock API response. Integration test: mock exchange rate API, trigger sync_rates → verify rates stored in DB and Valkey. Verify Valkey TTL is ~6 hours. GET /api/v1/currency/rates → returns current rates. Test get_rate: populate Valkey with rate → returns cached value. Clear Valkey → falls back to DB. Test background task doesn't crash on API failure (mock 500 response).

subtasks.title: Implement customer vetting pipeline with external API integrations and scoring algorithm
subtasks.description: Build the async vetting pipeline that orchestrates calls to OpenCorporates, LinkedIn, Google Reviews, and credit signal APIs with 10s timeouts, implements the weighted scoring algorithm (GREEN/YELLOW/RED), and exposes the vetting endpoints.
subtasks.details:
1. In `src/vetting/services/opencorporates.rs`: implement `check_business(org_name: &str) -> Result<OpenCorporatesResult>` using reqwest with 10s timeout. Query OpenCorporates API for business registration, good standing, directors. Parse response into struct: {verified: bool, data: serde_json::Value}. Handle timeout/error gracefully by returning unverified with error flag.
2. In `src/vetting/services/linkedin.rs`: implement `check_company(org_name: &str) -> Result<LinkedInResult>` with 10s timeout. Return {exists: bool, followers: Option<i32>}.
3. In `src/vetting/services/google_reviews.rs`: implement `check_reviews(org_name: &str) -> Result<GoogleReviewsResult>` with 10s timeout. Return {rating: Option<f32>, count: Option<i32>}.
4. In `src/vetting/services/credit.rs`: implement `check_credit(org_id: Uuid) -> Result<CreditResult>` with 10s timeout. Store raw signal in vetting.credit_signals. Return {score: Option<i32>}.
5. In `src/vetting/pipeline.rs`: implement `run_pipeline(org_id: Uuid, org_name: &str, pool, valkey)` that:
   - Updates vetting_jobs status to 'running'.
   - Runs all 4 checks concurrently using tokio::join! (they're independent).
   - Calculates weighted score: business_verified (30%: 100 if verified, 0 if not), online_presence (20%: linkedin_exists 50 + min(followers/100, 50)), reputation (25%: google_rating/5 * 80 + min(review_count/10, 20)), credit (25%: credit_score normalized to 0-100).
   - Map total: >=80 GREEN, 50-79 YELLOW, <50 RED.
   - Insert vetting_results row. Cache in Valkey with key `vetting:{org_id}` TTL 24h.
   - Update vetting_jobs status to 'completed' or 'failed'.
6. In `src/vetting/handlers.rs`:
   - `POST /api/v1/vetting/run`: accept {org_id, org_name}. Create vetting_jobs row. Spawn tokio::spawn(run_pipeline(...)). Return 202 {job_id}.
   - `GET /api/v1/vetting/:org_id`: check Valkey cache first, fallback to DB. Return latest vetting_results. 404 if none.
   - `GET /api/v1/vetting/credit/:org_id`: query vetting.credit_signals for org. Return array.
7. Wire routes.
subtasks.test_strategy: Integration test full pipeline: mock all 4 APIs (OpenCorporates verified, LinkedIn exists with 500 followers, Google 4.5 rating with 50 reviews, credit score 750) → verify final_score=GREEN with correct weighted calculation. Mock OpenCorporates not found, others good → verify final_score drops significantly. Mock all APIs failing → verify graceful handling, final_score=RED. Timeout test: mock one API with 15s delay → verify 10s timeout triggers, pipeline completes with partial data. POST /api/v1/vetting/run → 202 returned immediately. GET /api/v1/vetting/:org_id after pipeline completes → results returned. Verify concurrent execution: total pipeline time should be ~max(individual times), not sum.

subtasks.title: Implement automated payment reminders via NATS and audit event publishing
subtasks.description: Build the daily background task that checks for overdue invoices and publishes payment reminder events to NATS, plus audit event publishing for all finance and vetting operations.
subtasks.details:
1. In `src/finance/tasks.rs` (extend from currency sync): add a background task that runs daily (tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(86400))). Query finance.invoices WHERE status='sent' AND due_at < now(). For each overdue invoice:
   - Update status to 'overdue' if not already.
   - Publish to NATS subject `sigma1.notifications.payment_reminder` with JSON payload: {invoice_id, org_id, invoice_number, total_cents, due_at, days_overdue}. Fire-and-forget (per D3).
2. In `src/shared/audit.rs`: implement `publish_audit_event(nats: &async_nats::Client, domain: &str, action: &str, entity_id: Uuid, data: Value)`. Publishes to `sigma1.audit.{domain}.{action}` with JSON payload: {entity_id, action, data, timestamp}.
3. Integrate audit publishing into finance handlers: invoice.created, invoice.sent, invoice.paid, payment.recorded, payroll.created.
4. Integrate audit publishing into vetting handlers: vetting.started, vetting.completed.
5. Ensure NATS publish errors are logged but don't fail the request (fire-and-forget pattern).
subtasks.test_strategy: Test payment reminders: create invoice due 5 days ago with status 'sent'. Trigger reminder task. Verify NATS message published to sigma1.notifications.payment_reminder with correct payload. Verify invoice status updated to 'overdue'. Verify invoice due in future is NOT flagged. Test audit events: create invoice → verify NATS message on sigma1.audit.finance.invoice.created. Complete vetting → verify sigma1.audit.vetting.vetting.completed. Test NATS unavailable: verify operations still succeed (fire-and-forget), error logged.

subtasks.title: Implement GDPR customer deletion endpoint across both schemas
subtasks.description: Build the DELETE /api/v1/gdpr/customer/{id} endpoint that removes all customer data from both finance and vetting schemas (invoices, payments, payroll entries, vetting results, credit signals) within a single transaction, with audit logging.
subtasks.details:
1. In `src/shared/handlers/gdpr.rs`: implement `DELETE /api/v1/gdpr/customer/{id}`:
   - Accept customer org_id as path parameter.
   - Begin a database transaction.
   - Delete from finance.payments WHERE invoice_id IN (SELECT id FROM finance.invoices WHERE org_id = $1).
   - Delete from finance.invoices WHERE org_id = $1.
   - Delete from finance.payroll_entries WHERE employee_id IN (any employee linked to org — may need a lookup or accept employee_ids in request body).
   - Delete from vetting.credit_signals WHERE org_id = $1.
   - Delete from vetting.vetting_results WHERE org_id = $1.
   - Delete from vetting.vetting_jobs WHERE org_id = $1.
   - Commit transaction.
   - Publish audit event to sigma1.audit.gdpr.customer_deleted with {org_id, deleted_tables, record_counts}.
   - Invalidate Valkey caches: delete keys `vetting:{org_id}`, any invoice-related caches.
   - Return 200 with summary {deleted_invoices: N, deleted_payments: N, deleted_vetting_results: N, ...}.
2. This endpoint MUST be behind admin allowlist middleware.
3. Wire route under admin-protected group.
subtasks.test_strategy: Create invoices, payments, vetting results, and credit signals for a test org_id. Call DELETE /api/v1/gdpr/customer/{id}. Verify all records removed from both schemas (SELECT COUNT = 0 for each table). Verify transaction: if one delete fails, none are committed (mock DB error mid-transaction). Verify audit event published with correct counts. Verify Valkey cache invalidated. Verify non-admin user gets 403.

subtasks.title: Build Dockerfile and Kubernetes deployment manifests
subtasks.description: Create a multi-stage Dockerfile for the finance-vetting binary and Kubernetes manifests (Deployment, Service) for the sigma1 namespace with envFrom sigma1-infra-endpoints.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `Dockerfile`:
   - Stage 1 (builder): FROM rust:1.75-bookworm. Copy Cargo.toml/lock and src/. Run `cargo build --release`. Output: `target/release/finance-vetting`.
   - Stage 2 (runtime): FROM debian:bookworm-slim. Install ca-certificates, libssl3. Copy binary from builder. EXPOSE 8080. ENTRYPOINT ["./finance-vetting"].
2. Create `k8s/deployment.yaml`:
   - apiVersion apps/v1, kind Deployment, name finance-vetting, namespace sigma1.
   - replicas: 2.
   - Container: image from registry, port 8080.
   - envFrom: [{configMapRef: {name: sigma1-infra-endpoints}}].
   - Additional env vars: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY (from Secret), STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET (from Secret), ADMIN_ALLOWLIST (from ConfigMap).
   - Liveness probe: httpGet /health/live, port 8080, initialDelaySeconds 10, periodSeconds 30.
   - Readiness probe: httpGet /health/ready, port 8080, initialDelaySeconds 15, periodSeconds 10.
   - Resources: requests 128Mi/100m, limits 512Mi/500m.
3. Create `k8s/service.yaml`: ClusterIP service, port 80 → targetPort 8080, selector matching deployment.
4. Create `k8s/secret.yaml` template (placeholder): for STRIPE_SECRET_KEY and STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
5. Add `.dockerignore` to exclude target/, .git/, etc.
subtasks.test_strategy: Verify Dockerfile builds successfully: `docker build -t finance-vetting .` completes without errors. Verify resulting image size is reasonable (<150MB). Verify container starts and /health/live returns 200. Validate Kubernetes manifests with `kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f k8s/`. Verify envFrom references sigma1-infra-endpoints. Verify probes point to correct paths.

subtasks.title: Implement Valkey caching layer for vetting results and invoice dashboard counts
subtasks.description: Add Valkey caching for vetting results (TTL 24h), currency rates (already in 4009), and invoice summary counts for dashboard use, with cache invalidation on writes.
subtasks.details:
1. In `src/valkey.rs` (extend): add helper functions: `cache_get<T: DeserializeOwned>(conn, key) -> Option<T>`, `cache_set<T: Serialize>(conn, key, value, ttl_secs)`, `cache_delete(conn, key)`.
2. In vetting handlers: on GET /api/v1/vetting/:org_id, check Valkey key `vetting:{org_id}` first. If hit, return cached result. If miss, query DB, cache result with 24h TTL.
3. In vetting pipeline: after storing vetting_results, SET in Valkey `vetting:{org_id}` with 24h TTL.
4. In finance handlers: cache invoice dashboard counts in Valkey key `finance:dashboard:{org_id}` with 5-minute TTL: {total_invoices, draft_count, sent_count, paid_count, overdue_count, total_outstanding_cents}. Invalidate on invoice create/update.
5. Ensure all cache operations are best-effort: log errors but don't fail requests if Valkey is down.
subtasks.test_strategy: Test vetting cache hit: populate Valkey with vetting result, GET endpoint → returns cached data without DB query (verify with DB query count). Test cache miss: empty Valkey, GET endpoint → queries DB, result now in Valkey. Test cache invalidation: run new vetting pipeline → old cache replaced. Test dashboard cache: create invoices, first GET triggers DB query and cache SET. Second GET within 5min → cache hit. Create new invoice → cache invalidated. Test Valkey down: verify all operations still work via DB fallback.

title: Build Social Media Engine (Nova - Node.js/Elysia+Effect)
description: Implement the Social Media Engine for automated content curation, AI-powered caption generation, multi-platform publishing (Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, Facebook), and approval workflow. Uses Effect for service composition, retry logic, and schema validation. Owns the `social` schema in PostgreSQL.
details:
1. Initialize Bun project with Elysia 1.x and Effect 3.x. Dependencies: `elysia`, `effect`, `@effect/schema`, `@aws-sdk/client-s3` (R2), `pg` or `drizzle-orm` (PostgreSQL), `ioredis` (Valkey), `openai` (caption generation), `prom-client`.
2. Database migrations for `social` schema: `drafts` (id UUID, event_id, images JSONB array of {url, content_hash, width, height, ai_score}, caption text, platform_variants JSONB {instagram: {caption, crop}, linkedin: {caption, crop}, tiktok: {caption, crop}, facebook: {caption, crop}}, status enum draft/approved/rejected/published, created_at, approved_at, published_at), `published_posts` (id, draft_id, platform, platform_post_id, published_at, engagement_metrics JSONB).
3. Implement endpoints per PRD:
   - `POST /api/v1/social/upload` — accept multipart image upload, store to R2 under `events/{event_id}/`, compute content_hash and dimensions, store metadata in `drafts` table. Trigger AI curation pipeline.
   - `GET /api/v1/social/drafts` — list drafts with status filter.
   - `GET /api/v1/social/drafts/:id` — draft detail with all platform variants.
   - `POST /api/v1/social/drafts/:id/approve` — mark approved (admin allowlist per D11).
   - `POST /api/v1/social/drafts/:id/reject` — mark rejected with reason.
   - `POST /api/v1/social/drafts/:id/publish` — publish to specified platforms (admin only).
   - `GET /api/v1/social/published` — list published posts with engagement metrics.
   - `DELETE /api/v1/gdpr/customer/{id}` — delete all social content related to customer events.
   - `GET /metrics` — Prometheus metrics.
   - `GET /health/live`, `GET /health/ready`.
4. AI Curation Pipeline (triggered on upload):
   - Score each image using OpenAI vision API (composition, lighting, equipment visibility, brand relevance) → ai_score 0-100.
   - Select top 5-10 images per event.
   - Generate platform-specific crops: Instagram square (1080x1080) + Story (1080x1920), LinkedIn landscape (1200x627), TikTok (1080x1920).
   - Generate captions using OpenAI/Claude: include event context, featured equipment, relevant hashtags. Generate platform-specific variants.
5. Effect integration:
   - Define Effect Services: `InstagramService`, `LinkedInService`, `TikTokService`, `FacebookService` as Effect.Service tags.
   - Use `Effect.retry` with exponential backoff (base 1s, max 30s, 3 attempts) for all platform API calls.
   - Use `@effect/schema` for all request/response validation.
   - Use `Effect.gen` for pipeline composition (upload → score → crop → caption → draft creation).
6. Multi-platform publishing implementation:
   - Instagram: Graph API for feed posts, separate Story upload.
   - LinkedIn: Share API with image upload.
   - Facebook: Graph API page post.
   - TikTok: Video/image share API.
   - Each platform publish is an independent Effect that can fail without blocking others.
7. Approval workflow: drafts created in `draft` status → Morgan sends preview via Signal to Mike → Mike approves/rejects via Signal (handled by Morgan agent in Task 7) → approve/reject endpoint called.
8. Portfolio sync: published posts with high engagement (configurable threshold) automatically tagged for website portfolio (consumed by Task 8).
9. Valkey: cache published post lists (TTL 5min), rate limit platform API calls.
10. NATS: publish to `sigma1.audit.social.*` on publish events.
11. Admin allowlist middleware per D11.
12. Dockerfile: Bun-based image.
13. Kubernetes Deployment: namespace `sigma1`, 1 replica (medium priority), envFrom `sigma1-infra-endpoints`.
test_strategy: 1. Unit test AI curation scoring: mock OpenAI vision API response, verify top-N selection logic picks highest-scored images. 2. Unit test caption generation: mock OpenAI, verify platform-specific variants generated (Instagram has hashtags, LinkedIn is professional tone). 3. Integration test upload → draft creation: upload 3 images, verify draft created with 3 image entries, each with content_hash and dimensions. 4. Integration test approval workflow: create draft, approve, verify status=approved and approved_at set. 5. Integration test publish: mock Instagram Graph API, publish approved draft, verify published_posts record created with platform_post_id. 6. Effect retry test: mock Instagram API to fail twice then succeed, verify Effect.retry completes successfully after 3 attempts. 7. Verify @effect/schema validation rejects malformed upload requests with 422 error. 8. GDPR test: upload images for customer event, call DELETE endpoint, verify drafts and published posts deleted. 9. Verify /health/ready returns 503 when PostgreSQL is unreachable. 10. Verify admin-only endpoints return 403 without allowlisted identity.
decision_points: OpenAI vs Claude for vision scoring and caption generation — OpenAI has vision API (GPT-4o), Claude has multimodal support; which to use as primary, and should there be a fallback?
Image cropping/resizing library choice — sharp (native), Jimp (pure JS), or offload to a serverless image processing function (e.g., Cloudflare Image Resizing)?
PostgreSQL ORM choice — drizzle-orm vs raw pg client; drizzle provides type-safe queries and migration tooling but adds a dependency
Platform API authentication strategy — how are OAuth tokens for Instagram/Facebook/LinkedIn/TikTok stored and refreshed? Stored in Vault/secrets or managed via a token refresh service?
Portfolio sync mechanism — should high-engagement posts be flagged via a column in published_posts and polled by Task 8, or should a NATS event be published for real-time consumption?
TikTok API access — TikTok's Content Posting API has strict approval requirements; confirm whether API access is already provisioned or if a stub/mock should be built for v1

subtasks.title: Scaffold Bun/Elysia/Effect project with configuration and dependencies
subtasks.description: Initialize the Bun project with Elysia 1.x and Effect 3.x, install all required dependencies, configure TypeScript, and set up the project structure with environment variable loading from sigma1-infra-endpoints.
subtasks.details:
1. Run `bun init` and install dependencies: `elysia`, `effect`, `@effect/schema`, `@aws-sdk/client-s3`, `drizzle-orm` (or chosen ORM), `ioredis`, `openai`, `prom-client`, `sharp` (for image processing).
2. Configure `tsconfig.json` with strict mode and Effect-compatible settings.
3. Create project structure: `src/routes/`, `src/services/`, `src/db/`, `src/effects/`, `src/middleware/`, `src/config/`.
4. Create `src/config/env.ts` that reads from environment variables (DATABASE_URL, VALKEY_URL, R2_ENDPOINT, R2_ACCESS_KEY, R2_SECRET_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, NATS_URL).
5. Create Elysia app entry point (`src/index.ts`) with basic server startup.
6. Set up `GET /health/live` returning 200 and `GET /health/ready` that checks PostgreSQL and Valkey connectivity.
7. Set up `GET /metrics` endpoint using prom-client with default metrics + custom request duration histogram.
subtasks.test_strategy: Verify `bun run` starts the server without errors. Confirm /health/live returns 200. Confirm /metrics returns Prometheus text format. Confirm TypeScript compiles with zero errors.

subtasks.title: Create PostgreSQL migrations for social schema (drafts and published_posts tables)
subtasks.description: Write and apply database migrations for the `social` schema including the `drafts` table with JSONB columns for images and platform_variants, status enum, and the `published_posts` table with engagement_metrics JSONB.
subtasks.details:
1. Create migration files in `src/db/migrations/`.
2. Migration 001: `CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS social;`
3. Migration 002: Create enum type `social.draft_status` with values: draft, approved, rejected, published.
4. Migration 003: Create `social.drafts` table: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), event_id UUID NOT NULL, images JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]' (array of {url, content_hash, width, height, ai_score}), caption TEXT, platform_variants JSONB (keys: instagram, linkedin, tiktok, facebook, each with caption and crop sub-objects), status social.draft_status DEFAULT 'draft', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Add indexes on event_id, status, created_at.
5. Migration 004: Create `social.published_posts` table: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), draft_id UUID REFERENCES social.drafts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, platform TEXT NOT NULL, platform_post_id TEXT, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), engagement_metrics JSONB DEFAULT '{}'. Add index on draft_id, platform.
6. Create drizzle schema definitions in `src/db/schema.ts` matching the migration.
7. Create a migration runner script.
subtasks.test_strategy: Run migrations against a test PostgreSQL instance. Verify tables exist with correct columns, types, constraints, and indexes via `\d social.drafts` and `\d social.published_posts`. Insert a sample row into each table and verify JSONB columns accept the expected structure.

subtasks.title: Implement R2 image upload and multipart upload endpoint
subtasks.description: Build the `POST /api/v1/social/upload` endpoint that accepts multipart image uploads, stores them to R2 under `events/{event_id}/`, computes content_hash and image dimensions, and creates initial draft records in the database.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/services/r2.ts` — an R2StorageService using @aws-sdk/client-s3 configured for R2 endpoint. Methods: `uploadImage(eventId: string, file: Buffer, filename: string): Promise<{url, key}>`, `deleteImages(keys: string[]): Promise<void>`.
2. Create `src/services/image.ts` — utility functions: `computeContentHash(buffer: Buffer): string` (SHA-256 hex), `getImageDimensions(buffer: Buffer): {width, height}` using sharp.
3. Create `src/routes/social/upload.ts` — Elysia route for `POST /api/v1/social/upload`:
   - Accept multipart form data with `event_id` field and multiple `images` files.
   - Validate file types (JPEG, PNG, WebP) and max size (20MB per file).
   - For each image: compute content_hash, get dimensions, upload to R2 at `events/{event_id}/{content_hash}.{ext}`.
   - Create a draft record in `social.drafts` with status='draft', images JSONB populated with url, content_hash, width, height (ai_score null initially).
   - Return 201 with draft ID and image metadata.
4. Use @effect/schema to validate the multipart request (event_id required, at least 1 image).
5. Return 422 for validation failures with structured error response.
subtasks.test_strategy: Integration test: upload 3 JPEG images with a valid event_id, verify 201 response with draft ID. Verify draft row in DB has 3 image entries with correct content_hash and dimensions. Verify R2 receives 3 PutObject calls with correct keys. Unit test: verify 422 returned for missing event_id, unsupported file type, and oversized file.

subtasks.title: Implement AI image scoring pipeline with OpenAI vision API
subtasks.description: Build the AI curation pipeline that scores each uploaded image using OpenAI's vision API for composition, lighting, equipment visibility, and brand relevance, then selects the top N images per event.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/services/ai-scoring.ts` — `ImageScoringService`:
   - Method `scoreImage(imageUrl: string, eventContext: object): Effect<{ai_score: number, reasoning: string}>` — calls OpenAI GPT-4o vision API with a structured prompt asking to rate composition (0-25), lighting (0-25), equipment_visibility (0-25), brand_relevance (0-25), returning total 0-100.
   - Parse the structured JSON response using @effect/schema.
2. Create `src/effects/curation-pipeline.ts` — Effect.gen pipeline:
   - Input: draft ID.
   - Fetch draft from DB, iterate over images array.
   - For each image, call `scoreImage` concurrently using `Effect.forEach` with `{concurrency: 3}`.
   - Update each image entry in the JSONB array with its ai_score.
   - Select top N images (configurable, default 5-10 based on total count; e.g., top 50% or max 10).
   - Update the draft's images array to only include selected images (or mark unselected).
3. Wire pipeline to be triggered after upload completes (called from upload route or via a small in-process queue).
4. Use `Effect.retry` with Schedule.exponential(1000).pipe(Schedule.intersect(Schedule.recurs(3))) for OpenAI API calls.
5. Handle OpenAI rate limits gracefully — catch 429 errors and apply longer backoff.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test: mock OpenAI vision API to return known scores for 5 images, verify top-3 selection picks the 3 highest-scored. Verify ai_score is written back to the draft's images JSONB. Unit test retry: mock OpenAI to fail twice with 429 then succeed, verify Effect.retry completes. Verify malformed OpenAI response triggers schema validation error.

subtasks.title: Implement platform-specific image crop generation
subtasks.description: Generate platform-specific image crops for each selected image: Instagram square (1080x1080) and Story (1080x1920), LinkedIn landscape (1200x627), TikTok vertical (1080x1920).
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/services/image-cropper.ts` — `ImageCropService`:
   - Method `generateCrops(imageBuffer: Buffer, originalDimensions: {width, height}): Promise<PlatformCrops>` where PlatformCrops = { instagram: {square: Buffer, story: Buffer}, linkedin: {landscape: Buffer}, tiktok: {vertical: Buffer}, facebook: {landscape: Buffer} }.
   - Use `sharp` library for resizing/cropping. Apply smart crop (sharp's `attention` strategy) to maintain subject focus.
   - Instagram square: resize to cover 1080x1080, crop center. Story: resize to cover 1080x1920.
   - LinkedIn landscape: resize to cover 1200x627.
   - TikTok vertical: resize to cover 1080x1920.
   - Facebook landscape: resize to cover 1200x630.
2. Create `src/services/crop-storage.ts` — upload each crop variant to R2 under `events/{event_id}/crops/{platform}/{content_hash}.jpg`. Return URLs.
3. Integrate into curation pipeline: after scoring and selection, generate crops for each selected image. Store crop URLs in the draft's platform_variants JSONB under each platform's `crop` field.
4. Output JPEG at 85% quality for file size optimization.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test: pass a 4000x3000 image buffer through generateCrops, verify output dimensions are exactly 1080x1080 (instagram square), 1080x1920 (story/tiktok), 1200x627 (linkedin), 1200x630 (facebook). Verify JPEG quality. Integration test: after pipeline runs, verify draft's platform_variants JSONB contains valid R2 URLs for each crop variant.

subtasks.title: Implement AI caption generation with platform-specific variants
subtasks.description: Build the caption generation service using OpenAI to produce platform-specific caption variants — Instagram (with hashtags), LinkedIn (professional tone), TikTok (casual/trendy), Facebook (community-oriented) — and store them in the draft's platform_variants JSONB.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/services/caption-generator.ts` — `CaptionGenerationService`:
   - Method `generateCaptions(eventContext: {event_name, date, location, equipment_featured}, topImages: ImageMetadata[]): Effect<PlatformCaptions>` where PlatformCaptions = { instagram: string, linkedin: string, tiktok: string, facebook: string }.
   - Call OpenAI (GPT-4o or GPT-4-turbo) with a structured prompt:
     - Instagram: engaging, include 15-20 relevant hashtags, emoji-friendly, mention featured equipment.
     - LinkedIn: professional tone, focus on business value, no hashtags (or 3-5 professional ones).
     - TikTok: short, trendy, use relevant trends/sounds references, casual tone.
     - Facebook: community-focused, conversational, include call-to-action.
   - Parse response using @effect/schema to validate all 4 variants returned.
2. Integrate into curation pipeline after image scoring: call generateCaptions with event context.
3. Update draft's `caption` field with Instagram variant (primary) and `platform_variants` JSONB with each platform's caption under its `caption` key.
4. Use Effect.retry with exponential backoff for the OpenAI call.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test: mock OpenAI to return known captions, verify all 4 platform variants are stored in platform_variants JSONB. Verify Instagram caption contains hashtags (regex check for # symbols). Verify LinkedIn caption does NOT contain emoji. Integration test: trigger pipeline for a draft, verify caption field and platform_variants.instagram.caption, platform_variants.linkedin.caption, etc. are all populated and non-empty.

subtasks.title: Define Effect service layer and @effect/schema validation for all routes
subtasks.description: Create Effect.Service tag definitions for all platform services and the core services, implement @effect/schema schemas for all API request/response types, and wire up Effect.gen pipelines for route handlers.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/effects/services.ts` — define Effect.Service tags:
   - `InstagramService` — tag for Instagram Graph API operations.
   - `LinkedInService` — tag for LinkedIn Share API operations.
   - `TikTokService` — tag for TikTok Content API operations.
   - `FacebookService` — tag for Facebook Graph API operations.
   - `StorageService` — tag for R2 operations.
   - `AIService` — tag for OpenAI operations.
   - `DatabaseService` — tag for PostgreSQL operations.
   Each with interface defining their methods (publish, getMetrics, etc.).
2. Create `src/effects/layers.ts` — implement live layers for each service that connect to real APIs, and test layers that use mocks.
3. Create `src/schemas/` directory with @effect/schema definitions:
   - `upload-request.ts`: Schema for multipart upload (event_id: UUID, images: NonEmptyArray).
   - `draft-response.ts`: Schema for draft object with all nested types.
   - `approve-request.ts`: Schema for approval (empty body or optional comment).
   - `reject-request.ts`: Schema for rejection (reason: NonEmptyString).
   - `publish-request.ts`: Schema for publish (platforms: NonEmptyArray of enum).
   - `error-response.ts`: Standard error schema with code, message, details.
4. Create `src/effects/runtime.ts` — build the Effect runtime with all live layers composed.
5. Create helper `src/effects/elysia-bridge.ts` — utility to run an Effect inside an Elysia handler, catching Effect failures and mapping to HTTP status codes (validation → 422, not found → 404, unauthorized → 403).
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test: verify each @effect/schema rejects invalid input (e.g., non-UUID event_id returns parse error). Verify elysia-bridge maps Effect.fail with a ValidationError to 422 HTTP response. Verify service tags can be resolved from the composed layer without runtime errors. Verify test layers can substitute for live layers in unit tests.

subtasks.title: Implement draft management and approval workflow endpoints
subtasks.description: Build the draft listing, detail, approve, and reject endpoints with admin allowlist enforcement, including the complete approval workflow state machine.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/routes/social/drafts.ts`:
   - `GET /api/v1/social/drafts` — query social.drafts with optional `status` filter query param. Return paginated list (limit/offset). Use @effect/schema to validate query params.
   - `GET /api/v1/social/drafts/:id` — fetch single draft by UUID. Return 404 if not found. Include full platform_variants and images array.
2. Create `src/routes/social/approve.ts`:
   - `POST /api/v1/social/drafts/:id/approve` — admin-only (see middleware in 5012). Validate draft exists and status='draft'. Update status to 'approved', set approved_at=now(). Return updated draft.
   - `POST /api/v1/social/drafts/:id/reject` — admin-only. Validate draft exists and status='draft'. Accept JSON body with `reason` (required, non-empty). Update status to 'rejected'. Return updated draft.
3. Implement state machine validation: only 'draft' → 'approved'/'rejected', only 'approved' → 'published'. Return 409 Conflict for invalid transitions.
4. All routes use Effect.gen for composition and @effect/schema for validation.
5. Add Prometheus counter for draft state transitions (draft_transitions_total{from, to}).
subtasks.test_strategy: Integration test: create a draft, list drafts with status=draft filter, verify it appears. Approve it, verify status=approved and approved_at is set. Try to approve again, verify 409 Conflict. Create another draft, reject with reason, verify status=rejected and reason stored. Try to publish a rejected draft, verify 409. Verify 404 for non-existent draft ID. Verify 422 for reject without reason.

subtasks.title: Implement Instagram publishing via Graph API
subtasks.description: Build the Instagram platform service implementation using the Instagram Graph API for feed posts and Story uploads, wrapped in Effect with retry logic.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/services/platforms/instagram.ts` — implement `InstagramService` interface:
   - Method `publishFeedPost(imageUrl: string, caption: string): Effect<{platform_post_id: string}>` — uses Instagram Graph API: first POST to `/{ig-user-id}/media` with `image_url` and `caption` to create a container, then POST to `/{ig-user-id}/media_publish` with the container ID.
   - Method `publishStory(imageUrl: string): Effect<{platform_post_id: string}>` — similar flow but with `media_type=STORIES`.
   - Method `getPostMetrics(postId: string): Effect<EngagementMetrics>` — GET `/{post-id}/insights` for likes, comments, shares, reach.
2. Handle OAuth token refresh — read access token from environment, implement token refresh if expired (or document that token management is external).
3. Wrap all HTTP calls with Effect.retry using exponential backoff (base 1s, max 30s, 3 attempts).
4. Map Instagram API errors to typed Effect failures: `InstagramRateLimitError`, `InstagramAuthError`, `InstagramMediaError`.
5. Register as Effect.Layer implementing the InstagramService tag.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test with mocked HTTP: verify publishFeedPost makes correct sequence of API calls (create container → publish). Verify retry on 429 rate limit response. Verify InstagramAuthError raised on 401 response. Integration test (with mock server): full publish flow returns platform_post_id.

subtasks.title: Implement LinkedIn publishing via Share API
subtasks.description: Build the LinkedIn platform service implementation using the LinkedIn Share API for image posts, wrapped in Effect with retry logic.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/services/platforms/linkedin.ts` — implement `LinkedInService` interface:
   - Method `publishPost(imageUrl: string, caption: string): Effect<{platform_post_id: string}>` — LinkedIn flow: register image upload via `POST /rest/images?action=initializeUpload`, upload the image binary to the provided upload URL, then create a share via `POST /rest/posts` with the image URN and commentary text.
   - Method `getPostMetrics(postUrn: string): Effect<EngagementMetrics>` — GET `/rest/socialActions/{postUrn}` for likes, comments, shares.
2. Handle LinkedIn OAuth2 token — read from environment.
3. Wrap all HTTP calls with Effect.retry (exponential backoff, 3 attempts).
4. Map LinkedIn API errors to typed failures: `LinkedInRateLimitError`, `LinkedInAuthError`.
5. Register as Effect.Layer implementing the LinkedInService tag.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test with mocked HTTP: verify publishPost makes the 3-step flow (initialize upload → upload binary → create post). Verify correct headers (LinkedIn-Version, Authorization). Verify retry on 429. Integration test (mock server): full publish returns platform_post_id (URN).

subtasks.title: Implement Facebook and TikTok publishing services
subtasks.description: Build the Facebook Graph API page post service and TikTok Content Posting API service, each as independent Effect services with retry logic.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/services/platforms/facebook.ts` — implement `FacebookService` interface:
   - Method `publishPagePost(pageId: string, imageUrl: string, caption: string): Effect<{platform_post_id: string}>` — POST to `/{page-id}/photos` with `url` and `message` fields using page access token.
   - Method `getPostMetrics(postId: string): Effect<EngagementMetrics>` — GET `/{post-id}?fields=likes.summary(true),comments.summary(true),shares`.
   - Wrap with Effect.retry, map errors to typed failures.
   - Register as Effect.Layer for FacebookService tag.
2. Create `src/services/platforms/tiktok.ts` — implement `TikTokService` interface:
   - Method `publishImage(imageUrl: string, caption: string): Effect<{platform_post_id: string}>` — use TikTok Content Posting API: POST `/v2/post/publish/content/init/` with photo_images and title.
   - Note: TikTok API access may be pending; implement the full interface but include a feature flag to disable if credentials unavailable. Log a warning and return a TikTokUnavailable error if disabled.
   - Wrap with Effect.retry, map errors to typed failures.
   - Register as Effect.Layer for TikTokService tag.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test Facebook: mock Graph API, verify POST to /{page-id}/photos with correct fields. Verify retry on failure. Unit test TikTok: mock Content Posting API, verify correct request format. Verify feature flag disables TikTok gracefully (returns typed error, not crash). Verify both services register as valid Effect.Layers.

subtasks.title: Implement publish endpoint, admin allowlist middleware, and multi-platform orchestration
subtasks.description: Build the POST /api/v1/social/drafts/:id/publish endpoint that orchestrates publishing to multiple platforms concurrently using Effect, and the admin allowlist middleware per D11.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/middleware/admin-allowlist.ts`:
   - Read admin allowlist from environment variable (comma-separated identifiers) or config.
   - Elysia middleware that checks request identity (from auth header/token) against allowlist.
   - Return 403 with structured error if not in allowlist.
   - Apply to: approve, reject, publish endpoints.
2. Create `src/routes/social/publish.ts`:
   - `POST /api/v1/social/drafts/:id/publish` — admin-only.
   - Accept JSON body: `{ platforms: ['instagram', 'linkedin', 'facebook', 'tiktok'] }` validated with @effect/schema.
   - Validate draft exists and status='approved'.
   - For each requested platform, run the corresponding service's publish method as an independent Effect using `Effect.allSettled` (or `Effect.forEach` with `{concurrency: 'unbounded'}` and error recovery).
   - Each platform publish is independent — one failing does NOT block others.
