   - For each successful publish, create a `published_posts` record with platform, platform_post_id.
   - If ALL platforms succeed, update draft status to 'published' and set published_at.
   - If some fail, return 207 Multi-Status with per-platform results (or keep draft as 'approved' with partial results).
3. Create `GET /api/v1/social/published` — list published_posts with optional platform filter, join with drafts for context. Paginated.
subtasks.test_strategy: Integration test: create and approve a draft, publish to [instagram, linkedin]. Mock Instagram to succeed and LinkedIn to fail. Verify published_posts record created for Instagram only. Verify response includes per-platform status. Verify 403 returned for non-admin user. Verify 409 for publishing a non-approved draft. Verify GET /published lists the Instagram post with correct platform_post_id.

subtasks.title: Implement GDPR deletion, NATS audit events, Valkey caching, and portfolio sync
subtasks.description: Build the GDPR customer data deletion endpoint, NATS event publishing for audit trail, Valkey caching for published post lists, and portfolio sync tagging for high-engagement posts.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/routes/gdpr.ts`:
   - `DELETE /api/v1/gdpr/customer/{id}` — find all drafts where event_id belongs to the customer (requires event-to-customer mapping, may need to accept event_ids in request body or query an events service). Delete all associated R2 objects (originals + crops). Delete published_posts records. Delete drafts records. Return 200 with count of deleted items. Use a transaction.
2. Create `src/services/nats.ts`:
   - Connect to NATS using environment NATS_URL.
   - Method `publishAuditEvent(subject: string, payload: object): Effect<void>` — publish JSON to `sigma1.audit.social.*` subjects.
   - Publish events on: draft.created, draft.approved, draft.rejected, draft.published, gdpr.deleted.
   - Wire into route handlers after successful DB operations.
3. Create `src/services/cache.ts`:
   - Connect to Valkey using ioredis.
   - Cache `GET /api/v1/social/published` responses with key `social:published:{filters_hash}`, TTL 5 minutes.
   - Invalidate cache on new publish or GDPR deletion.
   - Cache `GET /api/v1/social/drafts` with TTL 1 minute.
4. Create `src/services/portfolio-sync.ts`:
   - After publishing, check engagement metrics periodically (or on-demand).
   - If engagement exceeds configurable threshold (e.g., >100 likes or >50 comments), tag the published_post as portfolio-worthy (add `portfolio_eligible: true` to engagement_metrics JSONB or a separate column).
   - Publish NATS event `sigma1.social.portfolio.eligible` with post details for Task 8 consumption.
subtasks.test_strategy: GDPR test: create drafts and published posts for a customer's events, call DELETE, verify all DB records removed and R2 deleteObjects called. NATS test: publish a draft, verify NATS message received on `sigma1.audit.social.published`. Cache test: call GET /published twice, verify second call returns cached response (mock DB to track query count). Invalidate on publish, verify third call hits DB. Portfolio test: mock engagement metrics above threshold, verify portfolio_eligible flag set and NATS event published.

subtasks.title: Create Dockerfile and Kubernetes deployment manifests
subtasks.description: Build the Bun-based Dockerfile for the Social Media Engine and create Kubernetes Deployment, Service, and ConfigMap reference manifests for the sigma1 namespace.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `Dockerfile`:
   - FROM oven/bun:1-alpine AS builder
   - WORKDIR /app
   - COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
   - RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile
   - COPY . .
   - FROM oven/bun:1-alpine AS runtime
   - WORKDIR /app
   - COPY --from=builder /app .
   - EXPOSE 3000
   - USER nonroot
   - CMD ["bun", "run", "src/index.ts"]
   - Include sharp binary dependencies (may need libc6-compat or build from source).
2. Create `k8s/deployment.yaml`:
   - namespace: sigma1
   - replicas: 1
   - Container with resource requests (cpu: 250m, memory: 512Mi) and limits (cpu: 1, memory: 1Gi) — higher memory for image processing with sharp.
   - envFrom: configMapRef sigma1-infra-endpoints
   - Liveness probe: GET /health/live, period 10s.
   - Readiness probe: GET /health/ready, period 5s, failureThreshold 3.
3. Create `k8s/service.yaml`: ClusterIP service on port 3000.
4. Add `.dockerignore` excluding node_modules, .git, test files.
subtasks.test_strategy: Build Docker image successfully with `docker build .`. Run container locally, verify /health/live returns 200. Validate Kubernetes manifests with `kubectl apply --dry-run=client`. Verify envFrom references sigma1-infra-endpoints. Verify image size is reasonable (<200MB).

title: Deploy Morgan AI Agent with MCP Tool-Server (Angie - OpenClaw/MCP)
description: Deploy and configure the Morgan AI agent on OpenClaw with all MCP tools wired to backend services via REST, Signal-CLI sidecar for messenger integration, ElevenLabs voice integration, Twilio telephony, web chat SSE endpoint, and all defined skills (sales-qual, customer-vet, quote-gen, upsell, finance, social-media, rms-*, admin). Morgan is the central orchestrator for all customer and business interactions.
details:
1. Create OpenClaw agent configuration for Morgan:
   - Agent ID: `morgan`
   - Model: configurable via env (default: `openai-api/gpt-4o` or as available)
   - Workspace: PVC `morgan-workspace` mounted at `/workspace`
   - System prompt: Define Morgan's persona as Sigma-1/Perception Events' AI business assistant. Include company context (lighting/visual production rental), operational guidelines, and tone (professional, helpful, knowledgeable about equipment).
2. MCP Tool-Server configuration — define all 11 MCP tools, each wrapping a REST call to the corresponding backend service (per D4):
   - `sigma1_catalog_search` → GET /api/v1/catalog/products?search={query} on equipment-catalog service.
   - `sigma1_check_availability` → GET /api/v1/catalog/products/{id}/availability?from={from}&to={to} on equipment-catalog service.
   - `sigma1_generate_quote` → POST /api/v1/opportunities on RMS service (creates opportunity with line items).
   - `sigma1_vet_customer` → POST /api/v1/vetting/run on finance-vetting service.
   - `sigma1_score_lead` → RPC ScoreLead via POST /api/v1/opportunities/{id}/score on RMS service (or compute from vetting results).
   - `sigma1_create_invoice` → POST /api/v1/invoices on finance-vetting service.
   - `sigma1_finance_report` → GET /api/v1/finance/reports/{type}?period={period} on finance-vetting service.
   - `sigma1_social_curate` → POST /api/v1/social/upload on social-engine service.
   - `sigma1_social_publish` → POST /api/v1/social/drafts/{id}/publish on social-engine service.
   - `sigma1_equipment_lookup` → GET /api/v1/equipment-api/catalog on equipment-catalog service (machine-readable).
   - Additional tool: `sigma1_get_vetting_results` → GET /api/v1/vetting/{org_id} on finance-vetting service.
   Each tool definition includes: name, description (for LLM), input JSON schema, REST method/URL/headers, response mapping.
3. Signal-CLI sidecar configuration:
   - Deploy Signal-CLI as sidecar container in Morgan's pod.
   - Mount signal-cli-config secret for phone number registration.
   - Configure Signal-CLI to listen for incoming messages and forward to Morgan via stdin/HTTP callback.
   - Morgan sends responses back via Signal-CLI's send API.
   - Handle photo attachments (download from Signal, store to R2, include URL in context).
4. ElevenLabs voice integration:
   - Configure voice model for Morgan (conversational, professional tone).
   - Implement SIP/PSTN bridge via Twilio → ElevenLabs → Morgan text pipeline.
   - Inbound call flow: Twilio receives call → streams audio to ElevenLabs STT → text to Morgan → Morgan response text → ElevenLabs TTS → audio back to caller.
5. Web chat SSE endpoint (per D14):
   - POST /api/v1/chat/message — accept user message, return 200. Morgan processes asynchronously.
   - GET /api/v1/chat/stream — SSE endpoint. Client connects, receives Morgan's streamed responses as SSE events. Events: `message` (text chunk), `tool_call` (tool being invoked), `done` (response complete).
   - Include session management: chat session ID in SSE connection, maintain conversation history in Valkey (TTL 1h).
6. Skill definitions:
   - `sales-qual`: Multi-turn conversation to qualify leads. Asks about event type, date, size, budget, location. Uses sigma1_check_availability and sigma1_catalog_search.
   - `customer-vet`: Triggers sigma1_vet_customer, interprets results, reports score to Mike via Signal.
   - `quote-gen`: Builds quote from conversation context, calls sigma1_generate_quote.
   - `upsell`: After quote, suggest insurance, additional services, package deals based on event type.
   - `finance`: Handle invoice requests, payment status checks, financial report queries.
   - `social-media`: Photo upload handling, draft review/approval flow via Signal.
   - `rms-*`: Project management, check-out/check-in, crew scheduling queries.
   - `admin`: Calendar queries (via RMS Google Calendar), email drafting, document lookup.
7. Conversation routing: Morgan detects intent from user message and activates appropriate skill. Multi-skill conversations supported (e.g., qualify lead → check availability → generate quote in one conversation).
8. Set X-Service-Identity: morgan-service header on all outbound REST calls (for admin allowlist per D11).
9. Health endpoint: Morgan pod exposes /health/live and /health/ready (checks Signal-CLI connectivity + backend service reachability).
10. Kubernetes Deployment in `openclaw` namespace: Morgan container + Signal-CLI sidecar, envFrom for API keys (ElevenLabs, Twilio, OpenAI), resource limits (512Mi/1000m request for Morgan, 256Mi/500m for Signal-CLI).
11. Cloudflare Tunnel route: configure route for web chat endpoints (/api/v1/chat/*) to Morgan service.
test_strategy: 1. Verify all 11 MCP tools are registered and callable: invoke each tool with sample input, confirm it reaches the correct backend service endpoint and returns valid response. 2. End-to-end Signal test: send a test message via Signal-CLI, verify Morgan receives it, processes it, and sends a response back within 10 seconds. 3. Web chat SSE test: POST a message to /api/v1/chat/message, connect to /api/v1/chat/stream, verify SSE events received with Morgan's response text. 4. Sales qualification flow test: simulate multi-turn conversation (event inquiry → date check → quote request), verify Morgan calls sigma1_catalog_search, sigma1_check_availability, and sigma1_generate_quote in correct sequence. 5. Customer vetting flow: send 'vet company XYZ' message, verify sigma1_vet_customer tool called and results interpreted correctly. 6. Voice integration test: mock Twilio webhook with audio transcript, verify Morgan processes and returns text response. 7. Verify Morgan sets X-Service-Identity header on all outbound calls. 8. Verify /health/ready returns 503 if Signal-CLI sidecar is down. 9. Verify conversation session persists in Valkey across multiple messages. 10. Response time test: simple query (e.g., 'what categories do you have?') completes in <10 seconds end-to-end.
decision_points: ElevenLabs voice pipeline architecture: Should Twilio stream audio directly to ElevenLabs via WebSocket (ElevenLabs Conversational AI), or should a custom intermediary service handle STT/TTS bridging? The former is simpler but may limit control over the Morgan<->voice pipeline.

Signal-CLI message forwarding mechanism: Should Signal-CLI forward messages to Morgan via HTTP callback (requires an HTTP server in Morgan's container) or via stdin pipe / shared filesystem? HTTP is more robust but adds complexity; stdin is simpler but harder to manage in a sidecar pattern.

OpenClaw agent framework specifics: Is OpenClaw a self-hosted framework or a managed platform? The MCP tool-server configuration format, agent lifecycle management, and deployment model need to be clarified to determine whether Morgan runs as a standalone container with an MCP SDK or as a plugin within an OpenClaw runtime.

Conversation session storage scope: Should Valkey store only web chat sessions (1h TTL) or also Signal and voice conversation history? Cross-channel session continuity (e.g., start on web chat, continue on Signal) requires a unified session model.

Photo attachment storage: Should Signal photo attachments be stored in Cloudflare R2 directly or proxied through a backend service (e.g., social-engine)? Direct R2 storage is simpler but bypasses any content moderation or cataloging logic.

subtasks.title: Configure OpenClaw agent definition for Morgan (agent ID, model, workspace PVC, system prompt)
subtasks.description: Create the core OpenClaw agent configuration file for Morgan, including agent ID 'morgan', configurable LLM model (default gpt-4o), PVC mount for workspace, and the comprehensive system prompt defining Morgan's persona as Sigma-1/Perception Events' AI business assistant with company context, operational guidelines, and professional tone.
subtasks.details:
1. Create the OpenClaw agent configuration YAML/JSON (format depends on OpenClaw's schema).
2. Set `agent_id: morgan`, `model: ${MORGAN_LLM_MODEL:-openai-api/gpt-4o}`.
3. Define PVC claim `morgan-workspace` (1Gi ReadWriteOnce) and mount at `/workspace` for persistent agent state.
4. Write system prompt covering:
   - Persona: Morgan, AI assistant for Sigma-1/Perception Events
   - Company context: lighting & visual production equipment rental
   - Capabilities: sales qualification, quoting, customer vetting, finance, social media, RMS operations, admin tasks

   - Operational rules: always identify as Morgan, escalate to Mike (owner) for decisions over threshold, never fabricate inventory/pricing
5. Set `X-Service-Identity: morgan-service` as default header for all outbound HTTP from the agent.
6. Configure conversation memory settings (context window management, summarization strategy for long conversations).
subtasks.test_strategy: Verify agent config loads without errors in OpenClaw runtime. Confirm agent ID resolves to 'morgan'. Confirm system prompt is included in LLM context. Confirm workspace PVC mounts successfully. Send a test message and verify Morgan responds with correct persona and tone.

subtasks.title: Define MCP tools for equipment-catalog service (catalog_search, check_availability, equipment_lookup)
subtasks.description: Create MCP tool definitions for the three equipment-catalog-backed tools: sigma1_catalog_search, sigma1_check_availability, and sigma1_equipment_lookup. Each tool definition includes name, LLM description, input JSON schema, REST method/URL/headers, and response mapping.
subtasks.details:
1. Define `sigma1_catalog_search` tool:
   - Description: 'Search the equipment catalog by keyword, category, or specifications'
   - Input schema: `{ query: string, category?: string, limit?: number }`
   - REST: GET /api/v1/catalog/products?search={query}&category={category}&limit={limit}
   - Response mapping: extract product list with id, name, category, daily_rate, specs
   - Header: X-Service-Identity: morgan-service
2. Define `sigma1_check_availability` tool:
   - Description: 'Check if a specific piece of equipment is available for given dates'
   - Input schema: `{ product_id: string, from: string (ISO date), to: string (ISO date) }`
   - REST: GET /api/v1/catalog/products/{product_id}/availability?from={from}&to={to}
   - Response mapping: available (boolean), conflicts (array), quantity_available (number)
3. Define `sigma1_equipment_lookup` tool:
   - Description: 'Get full machine-readable equipment catalog listing'
   - Input schema: `{ category?: string, page?: number, per_page?: number }`
   - REST: GET /api/v1/equipment-api/catalog?category={category}&page={page}&per_page={per_page}
   - Response mapping: structured product array with all fields
4. Set base URL from ConfigMap/env: `EQUIPMENT_CATALOG_URL`.
5. Register all three tools in MCP tool-server config.
subtasks.test_strategy: Invoke each tool with sample input: sigma1_catalog_search with query='LED', sigma1_check_availability with a valid product_id and date range, sigma1_equipment_lookup with no filters. Verify each tool makes the correct HTTP request to equipment-catalog service and returns properly mapped response. Verify X-Service-Identity header is present on all requests.

subtasks.title: Define MCP tools for RMS service (generate_quote, score_lead)
subtasks.description: Create MCP tool definitions for the two RMS-backed tools: sigma1_generate_quote and sigma1_score_lead. Each includes name, LLM description, input JSON schema, REST endpoint mapping, and response transformation.
subtasks.details:
1. Define `sigma1_generate_quote` tool:
   - Description: 'Generate a rental quote/opportunity with line items for a customer'

   - REST: POST /api/v1/opportunities

Invoke sigma1_score_lead with a valid opportunity_id, verify POST to correct scoring endpoint. Confirm response mapping extracts expected fields. Verify X-Service-Identity header on all requests.

subtasks.title: Define MCP tools for finance-vetting service (vet_customer, get_vetting_results, create_invoice, finance_report)
subtasks.description: Create MCP tool definitions for the four finance-vetting-backed tools: sigma1_vet_customer, sigma1_get_vetting_results, sigma1_create_invoice, and sigma1_finance_report.
subtasks.details:
1. Define `sigma1_vet_customer` tool:

   - REST: POST /api/v1/vetting/run

subtasks.title: Define MCP tools for social-engine service (social_curate, social_publish)

subtasks.title: Configure Signal-CLI sidecar container and message forwarding to Morgan
subtasks.description: Deploy Signal-CLI as a sidecar container in Morgan's pod, mount registration secrets, configure inbound message forwarding to Morgan, outbound response sending, and photo attachment handling with R2 storage.
subtasks.details:
1. Add Signal-CLI sidecar container to Morgan's pod spec:
   - Image: signal-cli-rest-api (or equivalent)
   - Resource limits: 256Mi memory, 500m CPU
   - Mount secret `signal-cli-config` at `/home/.local/share/signal-cli` (contains registered phone number data)
2. Configure Signal-CLI REST API mode:
   - Expose internal HTTP API on port 8080 within the pod (not externally)
   - Enable receive mode: daemon with JSON-RPC or HTTP callback
3. Message forwarding pipeline:
   - Configure Signal-CLI to POST incoming messages to Morgan's internal endpoint (e.g., http://localhost:3000/signal/incoming)
   - Message payload: sender number, message text, attachments list, timestamp, group info if applicable
4. Morgan → Signal response path:
   - Morgan calls Signal-CLI's send API: POST http://localhost:8080/v2/send with recipient number and message text
   - Support message formatting: bold, links, code blocks where Signal supports them
5. Photo attachment handling:
   - When Signal message includes attachments, Signal-CLI downloads them to a shared volume
   - Morgan uploads attachment to Cloudflare R2 via S3-compatible API
   - Include R2 URL in Morgan's conversation context for processing (e.g., social media posting)
6. Configure Signal-CLI health check: GET /v1/about should return 200.
7. Add shared emptyDir volume between Morgan and Signal-CLI for attachment transfer.
subtasks.test_strategy: 1. Verify Signal-CLI sidecar starts and /v1/about returns 200. 2. Send a test Signal message to the registered number, verify it arrives at Morgan's /signal/incoming endpoint with correct payload. 3. Call Signal-CLI send API with a test message, verify it's delivered. 4. Send a photo via Signal, verify it's downloaded, uploaded to R2, and R2 URL is accessible. 5. Verify sidecar resource limits are enforced.

subtasks.title: Implement ElevenLabs voice integration with Twilio telephony bridge
subtasks.description: Configure the voice pipeline: Twilio receives inbound PSTN calls, streams audio to ElevenLabs for STT, forwards text to Morgan for processing, sends Morgan's response text to ElevenLabs TTS, and streams audio back to the caller.
subtasks.details:
1. Configure Twilio phone number webhook:
   - Inbound call → Twilio sends webhook to Morgan's /api/v1/voice/incoming endpoint
   - Return TwiML to establish WebSocket media stream or forward to ElevenLabs Conversational AI
2. ElevenLabs Conversational AI setup:
   - Create voice agent in ElevenLabs with professional, conversational tone
   - Configure voice model ID and stability/clarity settings via env vars: `ELEVENLABS_VOICE_ID`, `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY`
   - Set up the agent's system prompt to mirror Morgan's text persona
3. Call flow implementation (Option A - ElevenLabs Conversational AI):
   - Twilio streams audio via WebSocket to ElevenLabs
   - ElevenLabs handles STT + TTS internally
   - ElevenLabs calls Morgan's text endpoint for LLM responses
   - Morgan processes and returns text, ElevenLabs converts to speech
4. Call flow implementation (Option B - Custom bridge):
   - Twilio WebSocket → custom bridge service → ElevenLabs STT API
   - Transcribed text → Morgan text processing
   - Morgan response → ElevenLabs TTS API → audio → Twilio WebSocket
5. Configure Twilio credentials via env: `TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID`, `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`, `TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER`.
6. Handle call events: call start, call end, caller hang-up, timeout.
7. Log all voice interactions with call SID for audit trail.
subtasks.test_strategy: 1. Mock Twilio inbound call webhook, verify TwiML/WebSocket setup response is valid. 2. Send sample audio transcript through the pipeline, verify Morgan receives text and returns response. 3. Verify ElevenLabs TTS is invoked with Morgan's response text. 4. Test call termination handling (caller hang-up). 5. Verify Twilio and ElevenLabs credentials are loaded from env. 6. End-to-end latency test: webhook to audio response under 5 seconds for simple queries.

subtasks.title: Implement web chat SSE endpoint with session management in Valkey
subtasks.description: Build the POST /api/v1/chat/message endpoint for receiving user messages and the GET /api/v1/chat/stream SSE endpoint for streaming Morgan's responses, with conversation session persistence in Valkey (1h TTL).
subtasks.details:
1. POST /api/v1/chat/message endpoint:
   - Accept JSON body: `{ session_id?: string, message: string, metadata?: object }`
   - If no session_id, generate one (UUID v4) and return in response
   - Store message in Valkey under key `chat:session:{session_id}:messages` (RPUSH)
   - Set/refresh TTL on session key to 1 hour
   - Trigger async Morgan processing for this message
   - Return 200 with `{ session_id, message_id, status: 'processing' }`
2. GET /api/v1/chat/stream SSE endpoint:
   - Query param: `?session_id={session_id}`
   - Establish SSE connection with proper headers (Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache, Connection: keep-alive)
   - Stream events as Morgan processes:
     - `event: message` — data: `{ text: 'chunk of response' }`
     - `event: tool_call` — data: `{ tool: 'sigma1_catalog_search', input: {...} }`
     - `event: tool_result` — data: `{ tool: 'sigma1_catalog_search', result_summary: '...' }`
     - `event: done` — data: `{ message_id: '...', full_text: '...' }`
   - Handle client disconnect gracefully (close SSE, cleanup)
3. Valkey session schema:
   - `chat:session:{id}:messages` — list of message objects (role, content, timestamp)
   - `chat:session:{id}:meta` — hash with created_at, last_active, user_agent
   - All keys with TTL 3600s, refreshed on activity
4. Connect to Valkey using env: `VALKEY_URL`.
5. Support CORS headers for web client access.
6. Implement heartbeat: send SSE comment (`: keepalive`) every 15s to prevent connection timeout.
subtasks.test_strategy: 1. POST to /api/v1/chat/message with a test message, verify 200 response with session_id. 2. Connect to /api/v1/chat/stream with returned session_id, verify SSE connection established. 3. Verify SSE events arrive: at least one 'message' event and a 'done' event. 4. Send a second message in same session, verify conversation history is maintained. 5. Verify Valkey keys exist with correct TTL. 6. Wait >1h (or set short TTL for test), verify session expires. 7. Verify keepalive comments arrive every ~15s. 8. Test CORS headers are present.

subtasks.title: Implement skill definitions: sales-qual, customer-vet, and quote-gen
subtasks.description: Define the sales qualification, customer vetting, and quote generation skills within Morgan's agent configuration, including multi-turn conversation flows, tool invocation sequences, and response templates.
subtasks.details:
1. `sales-qual` skill:
   - Trigger: intent detection for event inquiries, rental requests, 'I need lighting for...'
   - Multi-turn flow: greet → ask event type → ask date/duration → ask venue/size → ask budget range → ask specific equipment needs
   - At each step, use sigma1_catalog_search to suggest relevant equipment
   - Use sigma1_check_availability to verify date availability for suggested items
   - Collect all qualification data into structured lead object
   - If lead is qualified, offer to generate a quote (transition to quote-gen skill)
2. `customer-vet` skill:
   - Trigger: 'vet company X', 'check out this client', or automatically for high-value quotes
   - Call sigma1_vet_customer with organization details from conversation context
   - Interpret vetting results: summarize risk score, flag any concerns
   - If high risk: notify Mike via Signal with summary and recommendation
   - If low risk: confirm to user and proceed with quote/booking
3. `quote-gen` skill:
   - Trigger: 'generate a quote', 'send me a quote', or natural transition from sales-qual
   - Gather required data from conversation: customer info, items, dates
   - Call sigma1_generate_quote with structured line items
   - Present quote summary to customer: items, dates, total, terms
   - Offer modifications: add/remove items, change dates
4. Define skill-specific prompt fragments that are injected into Morgan's context when skill is active.
5. Implement skill state tracking in conversation context (active_skill, skill_state, collected_data).
subtasks.test_strategy: 1. Sales-qual flow: send 'I need lighting for a wedding on March 15th', verify Morgan asks follow-up questions and calls sigma1_catalog_search. Provide date, verify sigma1_check_availability is called. 2. Customer-vet flow: send 'vet company Acme Events', verify sigma1_vet_customer is called and results are summarized. 3. Quote-gen flow: after sales-qual, say 'generate a quote', verify sigma1_generate_quote is called with collected data and summary is presented. 4. Multi-skill transition: complete sales-qual → quote-gen in one conversation without losing context.

subtasks.title: Implement skill definitions: upsell, finance, social-media, rms-*, and admin
subtasks.description: Define the upsell, finance, social media, RMS operations, and admin skills within Morgan's agent configuration, including trigger conditions, tool invocation patterns, and conversation flows.
subtasks.details:
1. `upsell` skill:
   - Trigger: automatically activated after quote generation
   - Logic: analyze event type and selected equipment, suggest complementary items
   - Suggestions: equipment insurance, delivery/setup services, backup units, package upgrades
   - Use sigma1_catalog_search to find complementary products
   - Present suggestions naturally in conversation, not as a hard sell
2. `finance` skill:
   - Trigger: 'create invoice', 'payment status', 'financial report', 'revenue this month'
   - Invoice creation: call sigma1_create_invoice with details from context or conversation
   - Reports: call sigma1_finance_report with appropriate type and period
   - Present financial data in readable format with key highlights
3. `social-media` skill:
   - Trigger: photo received via Signal, 'post this to Instagram', 'create social post'
   - Photo upload: call sigma1_social_curate with media URL and caption
   - Draft review: present draft to Mike via Signal for approval
   - Publish: upon approval, call sigma1_social_publish
   - Never auto-publish without explicit approval
4. `rms-*` skill (project management, check-out/check-in, crew scheduling):
   - These map to RMS service endpoints (may need additional MCP tools in future)
   - For v1: handle natural language queries about project status, equipment checked out/in, crew availability
   - Route complex RMS operations to the RMS web interface with a link
5. `admin` skill:
   - Calendar queries: 'what's on the schedule this week?' → query RMS Google Calendar integration
   - Email drafting: 'draft an email to client X about Y' → compose and present for approval
   - Document lookup: 'find the contract for event X' → search workspace documents
6. Define intent detection rules/examples for each skill to guide conversation routing.
7. Support graceful fallback: if Morgan can't determine skill, ask clarifying question.
subtasks.test_strategy: 1. Upsell: after a mock quote for a wedding, verify Morgan suggests relevant add-ons (insurance, setup). 2. Finance: send 'create an invoice for opportunity X', verify sigma1_create_invoice is called. Send 'revenue this month', verify sigma1_finance_report called with type='revenue'. 3. Social: send a mock photo URL with 'post this to Instagram', verify sigma1_social_curate is called and draft presented. 4. RMS: send 'what equipment is checked out?', verify appropriate response or link to RMS. 5. Admin: send 'what's on the calendar this week?', verify calendar query attempt. 6. Fallback: send ambiguous message, verify Morgan asks for clarification.

subtasks.title: Implement conversation routing and multi-skill intent detection
subtasks.description: Build the intent detection and conversation routing layer that analyzes user messages, activates the appropriate skill, and supports seamless multi-skill transitions within a single conversation.
subtasks.details:
1. Intent detection approach:
   - Leverage the LLM's native understanding via system prompt engineering
   - Include intent classification instructions in Morgan's system prompt with examples:
     - 'I need lights for an event' → sales-qual
     - 'Can you check if company X is reliable?' → customer-vet
     - 'Generate a quote for...' → quote-gen
     - 'Post this photo' → social-media
     - 'What's the revenue this month?' → finance
     - 'What's on the calendar?' → admin
   - For ambiguous intents, Morgan asks clarifying questions
2. Skill state machine:
   - Track active skill in conversation context
   - Allow skill transitions: sales-qual → customer-vet → quote-gen → upsell (natural flow)
   - Support skill interruption: user can switch topics mid-skill (e.g., ask about calendar during sales-qual)
   - Resume previous skill when interruption is handled
3. Multi-skill orchestration:
   - A single conversation can invoke multiple skills sequentially
   - Context from previous skills carries forward (e.g., customer details from sales-qual available in quote-gen)
   - Tool results accumulate in conversation history
4. Channel-aware routing:
   - Signal messages: may be terse, support photo attachments
   - Web chat: may be more verbose, support rich formatting
   - Voice: responses should be concise and natural-sounding
5. Store routing state in Valkey alongside conversation history.
subtasks.test_strategy: 1. Send a series of messages that naturally flow through multiple skills (event inquiry → check availability → vet customer → generate quote → upsell), verify each skill activates in sequence. 2. Mid-conversation skill switch: during sales-qual, ask 'what's on the calendar?', verify admin skill handles it, then verify sales-qual resumes. 3. Ambiguous message test: send 'help', verify Morgan asks for clarification. 4. Channel-specific test: verify Signal responses are concise, web chat responses use formatting.

subtasks.title: Create Kubernetes deployment manifest for Morgan pod with Signal-CLI sidecar
subtasks.description: Write the Kubernetes Deployment, Service, PVC, and related manifests for the Morgan agent pod in the 'openclaw' namespace, including the Signal-CLI sidecar container, resource limits, env vars, secrets, and health probes.
subtasks.details:
1. Kubernetes Deployment (`openclaw` namespace):
   - Deployment name: `morgan-agent`
   - Replicas: 1
   - Container 1 (morgan):
     - Image: configurable (OpenClaw agent image)
     - Resource requests: 512Mi memory, 1000m CPU
     - Resource limits: 1Gi memory, 2000m CPU
     - envFrom: secretRef for `morgan-api-keys` (OPENAI_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY, ELEVENLABS_VOICE_ID, TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER)
     - envFrom: configMapRef for `sigma1-infra-endpoints` (service URLs)
     - Ports: 3000 (HTTP for chat/voice endpoints)
     - Volume mounts: morgan-workspace PVC at /workspace, signal-attachments emptyDir at /tmp/signal-attachments
   - Container 2 (signal-cli):
     - Image: bbernhard/signal-cli-rest-api (or equivalent)
     - Resource requests: 256Mi memory, 500m CPU
     - Resource limits: 512Mi memory, 1000m CPU
     - Port: 8080 (internal only)
     - Volume mounts: signal-cli-config secret at /home/.local/share/signal-cli, signal-attachments emptyDir
   - Liveness probe: HTTP GET /health/live on morgan container, port 3000
   - Readiness probe: HTTP GET /health/ready on morgan container, port 3000
2. Service: ClusterIP exposing port 3000 (morgan HTTP)
3. PVC: morgan-workspace, 1Gi, ReadWriteOnce
4. Secret: morgan-api-keys (placeholder for all API keys)
5. Health endpoint implementation:
   - /health/live: return 200 if process is running
   - /health/ready: check Signal-CLI sidecar (GET localhost:8080/v1/about), check Valkey connectivity, return 200 if all healthy, 503 otherwise
subtasks.test_strategy: 1. Apply manifests to cluster, verify pod starts with both containers running. 2. Verify PVC is bound and mounted. 3. Verify env vars from secrets and configmaps are available in morgan container. 4. Verify /health/live returns 200. 5. Verify /health/ready returns 200 when Signal-CLI is up, 503 when Signal-CLI sidecar is killed. 6. Verify resource limits are enforced (describe pod). 7. Verify internal communication: morgan container can reach signal-cli on localhost:8080.

subtasks.title: Configure Cloudflare Tunnel route for web chat endpoints
subtasks.description: Add Cloudflare Tunnel configuration to route /api/v1/chat/* and /api/v1/voice/* traffic to the Morgan service within the cluster.
subtasks.details:
1. Add ingress rules to the existing Cloudflare Tunnel configuration:
   - Route: `/api/v1/chat/*` → `http://morgan-agent.openclaw.svc.cluster.local:3000`
   - Route: `/api/v1/voice/*` → `http://morgan-agent.openclaw.svc.cluster.local:3000`
2. Ensure SSE compatibility:
   - Disable response buffering for /api/v1/chat/stream (SSE requires unbuffered streaming)
   - Set appropriate timeouts: idle timeout ≥ 300s for SSE connections
   - Ensure chunked transfer encoding is preserved
3. Configure Twilio webhook URL to point to the tunnel domain + /api/v1/voice/incoming.
4. Verify CORS is handled either at tunnel level or application level (prefer application level for flexibility).
5. Test that SSE events flow through the tunnel without being buffered or truncated.
subtasks.test_strategy: 1. Verify /api/v1/chat/message is accessible through the Cloudflare Tunnel domain. 2. Connect to /api/v1/chat/stream via SSE through the tunnel, verify events stream without buffering delay. 3. Verify /api/v1/voice/incoming is reachable from Twilio's perspective (or mock external request). 4. Test SSE connection survives for >60s with keepalive. 5. Verify SSL termination works correctly for all routes.

subtasks.title: End-to-end integration testing across all channels and skills
subtasks.description: Execute comprehensive end-to-end tests validating all 11 MCP tools, all three communication channels (Signal, voice, web chat), multi-turn skill flows, session persistence, and health endpoint behavior.

title: Build Sigma-1 Website (Blaze - Next.js 15/React 19)
description: Build the Sigma-1 public website on Next.js 15 App Router with equipment catalog browsing (533+ products), self-service quote builder, project portfolio gallery, Morgan web chat widget (SSE-based), AI-native optimization (llms.txt, Schema.org), and Cloudflare Pages deployment. Uses Effect for data fetching and form validation, shadcn/ui components, and TailwindCSS 4.
details:
1. Initialize Next.js 15 project with App Router, React 19, TypeScript 5.x. Install: `effect`, `@effect/schema`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `tailwindcss@4`, shadcn/ui CLI for component scaffolding.
2. Configure TailwindCSS 4 with Sigma-1 brand tokens (colors, typography — use professional/industrial theme appropriate for lighting/production company).
3. Scaffold shadcn/ui components: Button, Card, Dialog, Input, Select, Table, Badge, Tabs, Sheet, Command, Calendar, Avatar, Skeleton.
4. Implement pages per PRD:
   a. `/` (Home): Hero section with value proposition (AI-powered lighting rental), CTA buttons (Browse Equipment, Get a Quote, Chat with Morgan), featured categories, testimonial section, trust signals. Static content with ISR.
   b. `/equipment` (Catalog browse): Server component fetches categories from Equipment Catalog API. Client-side filtering by category, search, price range, availability dates. Grid layout with product cards (image, name, day_rate, availability indicator). Pagination. Use TanStack Query + Effect for data fetching with error boundaries.
   c. `/equipment/:id` (Product detail): Server component for SEO (fetch product data at build/request time). Display images (carousel from R2 CDN URLs), specs table, day rate, availability calendar widget (fetches /availability endpoint). Add-to-quote button. Use Effect Schema for API response validation.
   d. `/quote` (Self-service quote builder): Multi-step form — (1) Select products + quantities + dates, (2) Event details (venue, type, contact info), (3) Review & submit. Effect Schema for form validation. On submit, POST to RMS opportunities endpoint via Morgan or directly. Show confirmation with estimated total.
   e. `/portfolio` (Past events gallery): Fetch published posts from Social Media Engine API. Masonry grid layout with lightbox. Filter by event type. Show equipment used, venue, date.
   f. `/llms.txt` (Machine-readable): Static route returning plain text with company info, services, equipment categories, contact methods, API endpoints — optimized for LLM consumption.
   g. `/llms-full` (Full content dump): Static route with comprehensive equipment catalog data for AI agents.
5. Morgan web chat widget:
   - Floating chat button (bottom-right corner) expandable to chat panel.
   - POST messages to /api/v1/chat/message (Morgan's endpoint via Cloudflare Tunnel).
   - Connect to /api/v1/chat/stream via EventSource (SSE per D14).
   - Display streamed responses with typing indicator.
   - Support message history within session (stored in client state + Valkey session).
   - Responsive: full-screen on mobile, panel on desktop.
6. SEO & AI optimization:
   - Schema.org structured data on all pages (LocalBusiness, Product, Offer, Review).
   - OpenGraph and Twitter Card meta tags.
   - Sitemap.xml generation.
   - robots.txt with crawl directives.
7. Performance:
   - Image optimization: Next.js Image component with Cloudflare R2 CDN URLs.
   - Static generation for home, llms.txt, llms-full.
   - ISR for equipment pages (revalidate every 60s).
   - Dynamic imports for chat widget (reduce initial bundle).
8. Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance — proper heading hierarchy, alt text on all images, keyboard navigation, focus management in chat widget, color contrast ratios.
9. Cloudflare Pages deployment configuration: `next.config.js` with output: 'standalone', Cloudflare adapter, environment variables for API endpoints.
10. Environment config: API base URL for Equipment Catalog, Social Engine, and Morgan chat endpoints. All via Cloudflare Tunnel URLs.
test_strategy: 1. Verify `/equipment` page loads and displays products: fetch returns 533+ products, grid renders at least 24 product cards on first page. 2. Equipment filtering test: select a category, verify only products in that category displayed; search for 'LED', verify results contain LED products. 3. Product detail page: navigate to `/equipment/{id}`, verify product name, images, specs, and day_rate rendered correctly; availability calendar shows available dates in green. 4. Quote builder test: add 3 products, fill event details, submit; verify form validation rejects missing required fields; successful submission shows confirmation. 5. Chat widget test: open chat, send 'hello', verify SSE connection established and response streamed into chat panel. 6. Portfolio page: verify masonry grid renders with images from Social Engine API. 7. `/llms.txt` returns plain text with content-type text/plain. 8. Schema.org validation: run structured data testing tool on `/` and `/equipment/:id`, verify valid LocalBusiness and Product schemas. 9. Lighthouse audit: Performance score >= 90, Accessibility score >= 95 on home page. 10. Mobile responsiveness: chat widget renders full-screen on viewport 375px width; equipment grid switches to single column.
decision_points: Quote builder submission target: Should the form POST directly to the RMS opportunities API endpoint, or route through Morgan's chat/API layer for AI-assisted quote enrichment? This affects both the submission URL and the response handling logic.

Chat widget session management strategy: Should chat history be persisted purely in client-side state (React state/localStorage), or should it rely on server-side Valkey sessions with a session token cookie? The latter requires an additional API round-trip on widget open.

Equipment availability calendar data source: Does the /availability endpoint return per-day granularity or date-range blocks? This determines whether the calendar widget uses a day-cell renderer or a range-selection UI pattern.

Cloudflare Pages adapter choice: Use @cloudflare/next-on-pages (community, mature) or the newer Cloudflare Workers-based adapter? This affects build configuration, edge runtime compatibility, and ISR behavior on Cloudflare.

subtasks.title: Initialize Next.js 15 project with App Router, TypeScript, and dependency installation
subtasks.description: Create the Next.js 15 project scaffold with App Router, React 19, TypeScript 5.x, and install all core dependencies: effect, @effect/schema, @tanstack/react-query, tailwindcss@4, and shadcn/ui CLI. Configure tsconfig, next.config.js with standalone output, and set up the base directory structure (app/, components/, lib/, hooks/, types/).
subtasks.details:
1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest sigma1-website --typescript --app --tailwind --eslint --src-dir --import-alias '@/*'`.
2. Install dependencies: `bun add effect @effect/schema @tanstack/react-query tailwindcss@4`.
3. Install shadcn/ui: `npx shadcn-ui@latest init` — select New York style, CSS variables, and configure `components.json`.
4. Create directory structure: `src/app/`, `src/components/ui/`, `src/components/layout/`, `src/components/features/`, `src/lib/api/`, `src/lib/effect/`, `src/hooks/`, `src/types/`.
5. Configure `next.config.js`: set `output: 'standalone'`, configure `images.remotePatterns` for Cloudflare R2 CDN domain.
6. Create `src/lib/env.ts` with typed environment variables (NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAT_API_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SOCIAL_API_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_R2_CDN_URL) using Effect Schema for runtime validation.
7. Set up TanStack Query provider in `src/app/providers.tsx` wrapping the app layout.
8. Create `.env.local.example` documenting all required environment variables.
subtasks.test_strategy: Run `bun run build` — project compiles with zero TypeScript errors. `bun run dev` starts dev server on localhost:3000. Verify TanStack Query provider renders without errors by navigating to root route. Confirm all dependencies resolve in package.json.

subtasks.title: Configure TailwindCSS 4 brand theming and scaffold shadcn/ui components
subtasks.description: Set up the Sigma-1 brand design system using TailwindCSS 4 with custom color tokens, typography scale, and spacing appropriate for a professional lighting/production company. Scaffold all required shadcn/ui components and create shared layout components (Header, Footer, PageShell).
subtasks.details:
1. Configure TailwindCSS 4 in `src/app/globals.css` with CSS custom properties: primary (industrial blue/electric blue), secondary (warm amber/gold for lighting accent), neutral grays, success/warning/error states.
2. Define typography scale: headings using a bold sans-serif, body text for readability, monospace for specs/prices.
3. Scaffold shadcn/ui components via CLI: `npx shadcn-ui@latest add button card dialog input select table badge tabs sheet command calendar avatar skeleton separator dropdown-menu tooltip scroll-area`.
4. Create `src/components/layout/Header.tsx`: responsive nav with logo, links (Home, Equipment, Portfolio, Get a Quote), mobile hamburger menu using Sheet component.
5. Create `src/components/layout/Footer.tsx`: company info, quick links, contact details, social media links.
6. Create `src/components/layout/PageShell.tsx`: shared page wrapper with consistent padding, max-width, breadcrumbs slot.
7. Create `src/app/layout.tsx`: root layout with Header, Footer, Providers, metadata defaults.
8. Verify dark mode toggle is NOT included (professional B2B site — light mode only unless brand requires it).
subtasks.test_strategy: Visual inspection: dev server shows Header with navigation, Footer with links, brand colors applied consistently. All 15+ shadcn/ui components import without errors. TailwindCSS custom tokens (e.g., `bg-primary`, `text-secondary`) render correct colors. Mobile menu opens/closes on 375px viewport.

subtasks.title: Implement Effect-based API client layer and shared types
subtasks.description: Build a reusable API client layer using Effect for type-safe data fetching from Equipment Catalog API, Social Engine API, and Morgan chat endpoints. Define shared TypeScript types and Effect Schemas for all API responses used across the site.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/types/equipment.ts`: define TypeScript interfaces — Product (id, name, description, category, images[], specs{}, day_rate, availability), Category, ProductListResponse, AvailabilityResponse.
2. Create `src/types/portfolio.ts`: Post (id, title, images[], event_type, venue, date, equipment_used[]), PostListResponse.
3. Create `src/types/quote.ts`: QuoteItem (product_id, quantity, start_date, end_date), EventDetails (venue, event_type, contact_name, email, phone), QuoteSubmission.
4. Create `src/lib/effect/schemas.ts`: Effect Schema definitions mirroring all types above for runtime API response validation.
5. Create `src/lib/api/equipment.ts`: Effect-based functions — `getProducts(filters)`, `getProduct(id)`, `getCategories()`, `getAvailability(productId, dateRange)`. Each returns Effect<Response, ApiError>.
6. Create `src/lib/api/social.ts`: `getPosts(filters)` function.
7. Create `src/lib/api/chat.ts`: `sendMessage(sessionId, message)` POST function.
8. Create `src/hooks/useEquipment.ts`, `src/hooks/usePortfolio.ts`: TanStack Query hooks wrapping Effect API functions with proper error boundaries, stale times, and cache keys.
9. Create `src/lib/api/errors.ts`: typed error classes (NetworkError, ValidationError, NotFoundError) as Effect error types.
subtasks.test_strategy: Unit test each Effect Schema: pass valid API response mock → decodes successfully; pass malformed data → returns validation error. Unit test each API function with MSW (Mock Service Worker) intercepting HTTP calls — verify correct URL construction, query params, and response parsing. TanStack Query hooks return loading/error/data states correctly in test component.

subtasks.title: Build Home page with hero section, CTAs, featured categories, and ISR
subtasks.description: Implement the `/` (Home) page as a server component with ISR, featuring a hero section with the AI-powered lighting rental value proposition, prominent CTA buttons (Browse Equipment, Get a Quote, Chat with Morgan), featured equipment categories, testimonial section, and trust signals.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/app/page.tsx` as a server component with ISR (`export const revalidate = 3600`).
2. Hero section: full-width background (use placeholder industrial/lighting imagery via R2 CDN URL), overlay with headline ('AI-Powered Lighting & Production Rental'), subheadline, and 3 CTA buttons using shadcn Button — 'Browse Equipment' (link to /equipment), 'Get a Quote' (link to /quote), 'Chat with Morgan' (triggers chat widget open via client event).
3. Featured categories section: fetch top 6 categories from Equipment Catalog API via server component. Render as Card grid with category image, name, item count. Each links to `/equipment?category={slug}`.
4. Testimonial section: hardcoded testimonials (3-4) in a carousel or grid using Card components. Include client name, event type, quote text, star rating.
5. Trust signals section: company stats (years in business, events completed, equipment items), partner logos, certifications.
6. 'Chat with Morgan' CTA: create a client component wrapper that dispatches a custom event (`open-chat-widget`) that the chat widget listens for. Use `'use client'` boundary only for this button.
7. Ensure all images use Next.js Image component with proper width/height/alt text.
8. Add page-level metadata: title, description, OpenGraph tags via `export const metadata`.
subtasks.test_strategy: Navigate to `/` — hero renders with headline text visible, 3 CTA buttons present and clickable. Featured categories section shows 6 category cards (mock API or verify against real endpoint). 'Browse Equipment' link navigates to /equipment. 'Get a Quote' link navigates to /quote. Page metadata includes correct title and OG tags. Lighthouse performance score check on this page.

subtasks.title: Build Equipment catalog browse page with filtering, search, and pagination
subtasks.description: Implement the `/equipment` page with server-side initial category fetch, client-side filtering by category/search/price range/availability, responsive product card grid with pagination, and TanStack Query + Effect data fetching with error boundaries.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/app/equipment/page.tsx`: server component that fetches initial categories and first page of products. Pass as props to client component for interactivity.
2. Create `src/components/features/equipment/EquipmentGrid.tsx` ('use client'): receives initial data, manages filter/search/pagination state.
3. Implement filter sidebar/bar: Category dropdown (from API categories), search input with debounce (300ms), price range slider (min/max day_rate), availability date picker using Calendar component.
4. Product card component `src/components/features/equipment/ProductCard.tsx`: Image (R2 CDN URL, 16:9 ratio), product name, category badge, day_rate formatted as currency, availability indicator (green dot = available, red = unavailable). Links to `/equipment/{id}`.
5. Grid layout: 4 columns on desktop, 2 on tablet, 1 on mobile using TailwindCSS responsive grid.
6. Pagination: 24 items per page. Page controls at bottom using shadcn Button group. Use URL search params for page state (supports browser back/forward).
7. Use TanStack Query `useEquipmentList` hook for client-side refetching on filter change. Show Skeleton cards during loading.
8. Error boundary: show user-friendly error message with retry button if API fails.
9. URL-synced filters: reflect filter state in URL search params so filtered views are shareable.
10. ISR configuration: `revalidate = 60` for initial server-rendered page.
subtasks.test_strategy: Load `/equipment` — grid renders at least 24 product cards. Select a category filter — grid updates to show only products in that category. Type 'LED' in search — results contain LED products only. Change to page 2 — different products render, URL updates with `?page=2`. Click a product card — navigates to `/equipment/{id}`. On mobile viewport (375px) — grid shows single column. Loading state shows skeleton cards. Simulated API error shows error message with retry button.

subtasks.title: Build Equipment product detail page with image carousel, specs, and availability calendar
subtasks.description: Implement the `/equipment/:id` dynamic route as a server component for SEO with image carousel, specifications table, day rate display, interactive availability calendar widget, and add-to-quote button. Use Effect Schema for API response validation.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/app/equipment/[id]/page.tsx`: server component that fetches product data by ID. Use `generateMetadata` for dynamic SEO (title = product name, description = product description, OG image = first product image).
2. Create `src/components/features/equipment/ImageCarousel.tsx` ('use client'): swipeable image carousel using product images array. Thumbnails below main image. Uses Next.js Image with R2 CDN URLs. Handle single-image products gracefully.
3. Create `src/components/features/equipment/SpecsTable.tsx`: renders product specs object as a key-value Table using shadcn Table component. Handle varying spec keys dynamically.
4. Price display: prominent day_rate with currency formatting. Show 'Contact for pricing' if day_rate is null.
5. Create `src/components/features/equipment/AvailabilityCalendar.tsx` ('use client'): interactive calendar (shadcn Calendar) showing available/unavailable dates. Fetches availability data via `useAvailability(productId, month)` hook. Green = available, red/gray = unavailable. Allow date range selection for quote.
6. Add-to-quote button: stores selected product + date range in client-side quote context (React Context or Zustand). Badge count on nav quote icon updates.
7. Create `src/lib/context/QuoteContext.tsx`: React Context for managing quote items across pages. Provides `addItem`, `removeItem`, `items`, `itemCount`.
8. Breadcrumb navigation: Home > Equipment > {Category} > {Product Name}.
9. Related products section: show 4 products from same category at bottom.
10. Validate all API responses through Effect Schema before rendering.
subtasks.test_strategy: Navigate to `/equipment/{valid-id}` — product name, images, specs table, and day_rate render correctly. Image carousel: swipe or click thumbnail changes main image. Availability calendar: current month shows colored date cells, clicking a date range highlights selection. Add-to-quote: click button, verify QuoteContext itemCount increments, navigate to /quote and item appears. Breadcrumb links work. Invalid product ID returns 404 page. Dynamic metadata verified via page source inspection.

subtasks.title: Build multi-step Quote Builder page with Effect Schema validation
subtasks.description: Implement the `/quote` page with a multi-step form: Step 1 — product selection with quantities and date ranges, Step 2 — event details (venue, type, contact info), Step 3 — review and submit. Use Effect Schema for comprehensive form validation and submit to the RMS opportunities endpoint.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/app/quote/page.tsx` with 'use client' directive for form interactivity.
2. Create `src/components/features/quote/QuoteBuilder.tsx`: multi-step form container with step indicator (1/2/3 progress bar using shadcn Badge/Tabs).
3. Step 1 — Product Selection (`QuoteProductStep.tsx`):
   - Display items from QuoteContext (added from product detail pages).
   - Allow manual product search/add via Command component (autocomplete from API).
   - Each item row: product name, quantity Input (number), start_date/end_date Calendar pickers, per-item subtotal (quantity × day_rate × days), remove button.
   - Running total at bottom.
   - Validation: at least 1 product, quantity > 0, valid date range (end >= start).
4. Step 2 — Event Details (`QuoteEventStep.tsx`):
   - Form fields: venue name (Input), venue address (Input), event type (Select: concert, corporate, wedding, festival, theater, other), contact name (Input), email (Input), phone (Input), additional notes (Textarea).
   - All fields validated via Effect Schema: email format, phone format, required fields.
5. Step 3 — Review & Submit (`QuoteReviewStep.tsx`):
   - Summary of all selected products with quantities, dates, line-item pricing.
   - Event details summary.
   - Estimated total (sum of all line items).
   - Edit buttons to go back to Step 1 or 2.
   - Submit button: POST QuoteSubmission to API endpoint.
6. On successful submission: show confirmation Dialog with quote reference number, estimated total, expected response time.
7. On submission error: show error message, preserve form state, allow retry.
8. Create Effect Schema validators in `src/lib/effect/quote-schemas.ts` for each step.
subtasks.test_strategy: Navigate to `/quote` with items in QuoteContext — Step 1 shows products with quantities and dates. Change quantity and dates — subtotal recalculates. Proceed to Step 2 — submit with empty required fields, verify validation errors appear. Fill valid data, proceed to Step 3 — review shows correct summary. Submit — verify POST request sent with correct payload. Successful response shows confirmation dialog. Go back to Step 1 from Step 3 — form state preserved. Submit with 0 products — validation prevents progression.

subtasks.title: Build Portfolio gallery page with masonry grid and lightbox
subtasks.description: Implement the `/portfolio` page that fetches published posts from the Social Media Engine API and renders them in a responsive masonry grid layout with lightbox viewing, filtering by event type, and display of equipment used per event.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/app/portfolio/page.tsx`: server component with ISR (revalidate = 300) fetching initial posts from Social Engine API.
2. Create `src/components/features/portfolio/PortfolioGrid.tsx` ('use client'): masonry grid layout using CSS columns (3 on desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile) or a lightweight masonry library.

subtasks.test_strategy: Navigate to `/portfolio` — masonry grid renders with images from Social Engine API. Click event type filter — grid shows only matching events. Click a portfolio card — lightbox opens with full image, event details, and equipment list. Use arrow keys to navigate between items in lightbox. Press Escape — lightbox closes. subtasks.title: Build Morgan web chat widget with SSE streaming and responsive design
subtasks.description: Implement the floating Morgan chat widget as a dynamically imported client component with SSE-based streaming responses, typing indicator, session management, and responsive design (panel on desktop, full-screen on mobile).
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/components/features/chat/ChatWidget.tsx` ('use client'): floating button (bottom-right, fixed position) with chat icon and unread badge. Click toggles chat panel open/closed.
2. Create `src/components/features/chat/ChatPanel.tsx`: chat UI with header (Morgan avatar, name, status indicator), message list (scrollable), input bar (text input + send button).
3. Message display: user messages right-aligned (brand primary), Morgan messages left-aligned (neutral). Support markdown rendering in Morgan's responses (bold, links, lists).
4. SSE streaming implementation:
   - On send: POST message to `/api/v1/chat/message` with session_id and message text.
   - Immediately open EventSource to `/api/v1/chat/stream?session_id={id}`.
   - Process SSE events: accumulate streamed text tokens into current Morgan message.
   - Show typing indicator (animated dots) while stream is active.
   - On stream complete (`[DONE]` event or stream close), finalize message.
5. Session management: generate session_id (UUID) on first open, store in localStorage. Include in all API calls. Message history maintained in React state within session.
6. Responsive design: on desktop (>768px) — 400px wide panel, 500px tall, anchored bottom-right. On mobile (<=768px) — full-screen overlay with close button in header.
7. Dynamic import: in `src/app/layout.tsx`, use `next/dynamic` with `ssr: false` to load ChatWidget only on client, reducing initial bundle.
8. Listen for `open-chat-widget` custom event (dispatched by Home page CTA) to programmatically open the panel.
9. Auto-scroll message list to bottom on new messages.
10. Handle connection errors: show 'Connection lost, retrying...' message, implement exponential backoff reconnect for SSE.
subtasks.test_strategy: Click floating chat button — panel opens with input field. Type 'hello' and send — POST request fires, SSE connection opens, typing indicator appears, streamed response renders incrementally. After response complete, typing indicator disappears. Send another message — conversation history preserved. Close and reopen widget — session maintained (same session_id). On mobile viewport (375px) — panel renders full-screen. Home page 'Chat with Morgan' CTA opens the widget. Simulate SSE error — retry message appears. Dynamic import verified: ChatWidget not in initial server-rendered HTML.

subtasks.title: Implement SEO: Schema.org structured data, OpenGraph, sitemap, and robots.txt

subtasks.test_strategy: View page source of `/` — JSON-LD script tag contains valid LocalBusiness schema. View source of `/equipment/{id}` — contains Product schema with correct name and price. Run Google Structured Data Testing Tool or Schema.org validator on home and product pages — no errors. `/sitemap.xml` returns valid XML with all static pages and equipment product URLs. `/robots.txt` returns valid directives with sitemap reference. OG tags verified via social media debugger tools (og:title, og:image present).

subtasks.title: Implement /llms.txt and /llms-full AI-readable static routes
subtasks.description: Create the /llms.txt and /llms-full static routes returning plain text content optimized for LLM consumption, including company information, services, equipment categories, contact methods, and comprehensive catalog data.
